title: Server-side domain whitelist enforcement on all protected API routes
description:
## Why
The @agenticdream.com domain check only exists in the client-side ClientProvider (clients.context.tsx:31). Middleware and API routes never validate the email domain. A user with a valid Supabase JWT from any domain can call internal API routes.

## IMPORTANT: Candidate routes must stay unauthenticated
Candidates (interviewees) access the product WITHOUT any login. These routes must never have auth applied:
- Pages: /call, /result
- API: /api/register-call, /api/check-response, /api/get-call, /api/fn, /api/invitations and all sub-paths

## What to change
1. In src/middleware.ts: for non-public routes, after confirming session exists, also check session.user.email ends with @agenticdream.com — if not, treat as unauthenticated (redirect to /sign-in for pages, 401 for API).
2. For internal-only API routes (routes NOT in the publicRoutes list above), add a server-side helper call to verify @agenticdream.com. Internal routes include: /api/user, /api/create-interview, /api/delete-interview, /api/toggle-interview-status, /api/interviews, /api/interviewers, /api/create-interviewer, /api/generate-insights, /api/generate-interview-questions, /api/interview-jobs, /api/responses, /api/responses/emails, /api/get-dreamit-jobs, /api/analyze-communication, /api/feedback.
3. DO NOT add auth to: /api/register-call, /api/check-response, /api/get-call, /api/fn, /api/invitations, /api/deepgram-token, /api/upload-recording — these are called during candidate interview sessions.

## Edge cases
- Candidate accessing /call with no cookies: works as before, no auth required
- Internal user with valid @agenticdream.com JWT: no change
- External Google account trying to access /dashboard or internal API: 401/redirect
- Candidate hitting /api/deepgram-token during interview: must not require auth

## How to verify
1. Access /call without any session cookie — EXPECT: page loads normally
2. Access /dashboard without any session cookie — EXPECT: redirect to /sign-in
3. Call /api/register-call without auth — EXPECT: 200 (candidate flow works)
4. Call /api/interviews without auth — EXPECT: 401
5. Call /api/interviews with non-@agenticdream.com JWT — EXPECT: 401

comments.author: pm
comments.text:
GROOMED

User story: As a security-conscious operator, I want all protected server-side routes and middleware to enforce the @agenticdream.com domain whitelist, so that a valid Supabase JWT from any other domain cannot access user data or protected pages.

Acceptance criteria:

1. Shared server-side helper:
   - Create (or extend) a helper such as src/lib/get-auth-session.ts that: retrieves the current Supabase session, checks that session.user.email ends with '@agenticdream.com', and returns null (not the session) if either condition fails.
   - The helper must be usable by both middleware and individual API route handlers without code duplication.

2. Middleware enforcement — src/middleware.ts:
   - Replace any existing session check with the new helper.
   - A session with a non-@agenticdream.com email must be treated identically to a missing session: user is unauthenticated, redirect to /sign-in.
   - A missing session (first visit, no cookies) must continue to reach public routes without error.

3. API route enforcement — every route under src/app/api/ that handles authenticated user data (including at minimum /api/user, /api/get-call, /api/register-call):
   - Call the shared helper on each request.
   - Return HTTP 401 (not 403, not 302) if the helper returns null.
   - Must not leak session data or user records in the error body.

Edge cases:
- First-visit / no cookies: helper returns null; middleware allows public routes unaffected; API routes return 401 (same as any unauthenticated call).
- Valid @agenticdream.com JWT: no behavior change — all existing routes continue to work.
- External Google account with a valid Supabase JWT (the bypass scenario): middleware redirects to /sign-in; API routes return 401.
- JWT present but expired (separate from domain check): handled upstream by getSession returning null — helper must not crash on null session.
- Case sensitivity: email check must be case-insensitive (@AGENTICDREAM.COM must also pass).

How to verify:
1. Obtain (or fabricate in a test) a valid Supabase JWT for a non-@agenticdream.com email (e.g. attacker@gmail.com).
2. Send a request to /api/get-call (or any protected API route) with that JWT in the Authorization/Cookie header.
3. EXPECT: HTTP 401 response. No user data in the body.
4. Attempt to navigate to /dashboard with that JWT in session cookies.
5. EXPECT: redirect to /sign-in, not the dashboard.
6. Repeat steps 2-5 with a valid @agenticdream.com JWT.
7. EXPECT: normal 200 / dashboard load — no regression.

comments.author: super
comments.text: CORRECTION: Candidate routes must NOT get auth enforcement. The GROOMED comment incorrectly lists /api/get-call and /api/register-call as routes to protect — these are called by candidates (interviewees) during their interview session and have no login. Do NOT add auth to: /api/register-call, /api/check-response, /api/get-call, /api/fn, /api/invitations (and sub-paths), /api/deepgram-token, /api/upload-recording. Only apply the @agenticdream.com check to these routes: /api/user, /api/create-interview, /api/delete-interview, /api/toggle-interview-status, /api/interviews, /api/interviewers, /api/create-interviewer, /api/generate-insights, /api/generate-interview-questions, /api/interview-jobs, /api/responses, /api/responses/emails, /api/get-dreamit-jobs, /api/analyze-communication, /api/feedback.

This single change upgrades all 14 routes already calling getAuthSession(). 2. src/middleware.ts — derive validSession after sessionError block: null if session email fails domain check; use validSession in isProtectedRoute and sign-in redirect checks. 3. src/app/api/responses/emails/route.ts — add getAuthSession() + 401 at top of GET handler (per CORRECTION; currently unprotected). 4. src/app/api/feedback/route.ts — add getAuthSession() + 401 at top of POST handler (per CORRECTION; currently unprotected). NOT touching: /api/get-call, /api/register-call, /api/check-response, /api/fn, /api/invitations, /api/deepgram-token, /api/upload-recording, or any public candidate-facing handlers (interviews/[id] GET, interviewers/[id] GET, responses POST, responses/[callId] GET/PATCH). Build gate: yarn build.

comments.text: DONE: Enforced @agenticdream.com domain whitelist server-side across all internal routes. Changes: (1) src/lib/route-auth.ts — getAuthSession() now returns null if email does not end with @agenticdream.com (case-insensitive); this single change upgrades all 14 routes that already call this helper; (2) src/middleware.ts — added validSession derived from session: null if domain check fails; used in place of session for isProtectedRoute and sign-in redirect checks; (3) src/app/api/responses/emails/route.ts — added getAuthSession() + 401 guard; (4) src/app/api/feedback/route.ts — added getAuthSession() + 401 guard. Candidate routes and public handlers left unchanged per CORRECTION. Build: yarn build passed (26/26 pages, middleware compiled). Commit: 126a5dc

comments.author: qa1
comments.text: PASS: all ACs met per GROOMED + CORRECTION. AC1 (helper): route-auth.ts returns null when session is missing or email does not end with @agenticdream.com (case-insensitive via .toLowerCase()); no crash on null session (optional chaining). AC2 (middleware): validSession derived inline — middleware cannot share route handler client — non-domain JWTs treated as unauthenticated for page routes; public routes unaffected. AC3 (API routes per CORRECTION): all 15 internal routes on CORRECTION's list have domain enforcement (13 pre-existing via getAuthSession upgrade, +2 added: /api/responses/emails GET and /api/feedback POST). Candidate routes verified: /api/register-call, /api/check-response, /api/get-call, /api/fn, /api/invitations, /api/deepgram-token, /api/upload-recording — none call getAuthSession (grep confirmed). Both new guards return 401, no session data leaked. Pre-mortem risks: (1) domain string duplicated between route-auth.ts and middleware.ts — technical constraint (different Supabase clients), consistent today, severity low; (2) /api/feedback and /api/responses/emails had 'Public' comments removed and auth added — CORRECTION mandates this; if candidate flow calls these routes it may regress, flagged for operator awareness; (3) responses/[callId] PATCH uses getAuthSession for field-level gating (not 401) — external JWTs now get CANDIDATE_FIELDS access instead of ADMIN_FIELDS, tightens security correctly.

title: Remove leftover /call/[id] route causing Next.js slug conflict
description:
## Why
Build is broken. Next.js throws: 'You cannot use different slug names for the same dynamic path (id !== interviewId)'. The [id] directory was not deleted when [interviewId] was added, leaving two conflicting dynamic segments at the same level.

## What to change
- Delete src/app/(user)/call/[id]/ and everything inside it (only file is page.tsx)
- Grep the codebase for any remaining hrefs, redirects, or router.push calls referencing /call/[id] or params.id in the call context and update them to use /call/[interviewId]/[jobId]/[applicationId]

## Edge cases
- Make sure no other dynamic route at the same level as [interviewId] exists after deletion
- If [id]/page.tsx had logic not carried over to [interviewId]/[jobId]/[applicationId]/page.tsx, port it before deleting

## How to verify
- Run: yarn build — it MUST pass locally before committing. Do not commit until build is green.
- No src/app/(user)/call/[id] directory exists
- git status is clean
- git push succeeds

comments.author: eng1
comments.text: PLAN: [id]/page.tsx has the full call UI (invitation-by-UUID, response check, Call component) — not in resolver. Fix: create [interviewId]/page.tsx (1-seg) from [id]/page.tsx (params.id→params.interviewId), then git rm [id]/. Resolver redirect /call/${invitation.id} will now hit [interviewId] 1-seg correctly. No href updates needed — existing /call/${slug} URLs already match the renamed segment. Run yarn build to confirm. Files: src/app/(user)/call/[id]/page.tsx → [interviewId]/page.tsx. Test: yarn build must exit 0.

comments.author: eng1
comments.text: DONE: Deleted src/app/(user)/call/[id]/page.tsx and created [interviewId]/page.tsx with params.id→params.interviewId. Both call routes now use the same first-segment name [interviewId], resolving the Next.js slug conflict. Build output confirmed: /call/[interviewId] and /call/[interviewId]/[jobId]/[applicationId] both compile without errors. Commit: b9e65ee. Pushed to main.

title: Show invalid-link error page for non-existent interview, test, or invitation
description:
User Story: As a candidate, I want a clear error page when I visit an invalid or inactive interview link, so that I understand why I cannot proceed.

Why: Candidates with stale or broken links see a crash or broken UI. This is a P1 trust issue at the most critical moment of the candidate experience.

Files to change:
- src/app/(user)/call/[interviewId]/page.tsx (primary fix)
- src/app/(user)/call/[interviewId]/[jobId]/[applicationId]/page.tsx (confirm existing not-found state only)

DB query (add after invitation is confirmed valid):
  supabase.from("interview").select("id,is_active,is_archived,is_deleted").eq("id", invitation.interview_id).maybeSingle()

Two new states to add to the existing state variable in /call/[interviewId]/page.tsx:
1. "interview_not_found" — data === null
2. "interview_inactive" — data.is_active === false OR data.is_archived === true OR data.is_deleted === true

Error UI: render using existing PopUpMessage component (already used for invitation not-found and expired states).

Copy — exact strings:
- Not found: title "This Link Is Not Valid", description "This interview may have been removed. Please contact the hiring team." image /invalid-url.png
- Inactive/archived/deleted: title "This Interview Is Closed", description "This interview is no longer accepting responses. Please contact the hiring team." image /closed.png

Do NOT use Next.js notFound() — these are client components. Error UI must be inline state, consistent with existing PopUpMessage pattern.

The /call/[interviewId]/[jobId]/[applicationId] route already checks invitation existence via application_id lookup. No interview check needed there — it redirects to /call/[invitationId] which will catch it. Only confirm existing "No Invitation Found" PopUpMessage renders correctly.

Edge cases:
- Interview fetch must happen AFTER invitation confirmed valid (not before).
- is_active === false alone is sufficient for "closed" state — do not require all three flags.
- If interview fetch returns a server error (not 404), show "This Link Is Not Valid" rather than proceeding to call.
- Existing "No Invitation Found" and "Invitation Expired" states and their copy must not change.

How to verify:
1. /call/nonexistent-uuid → "No Invitation Found" (existing, must not regress)
2. /call/expired-invitation-uuid → "Invitation Expired" (existing, must not regress)
3. Valid invitation + interview.is_active=false → "This Interview Is Closed"
4. Valid invitation + interview.is_deleted=true → "This Interview Is Closed"
5. Valid invitation with interview_id pointing to nonexistent row → "This Link Is Not Valid"
6. Valid invitation + active interview → proceeds to pre-call form normally

title: Audit and fix hardcoded color violations in src/ to Strata DS semantic tokens
description:
## User Story
As a developer, I want all hardcoded color classes in src/ replaced with Strata DS semantic tokens so that the codebase is fully compliant, dark mode works via tokens, and no raw Tailwind color classes remain.

## Why
Even after the CSS token foundation is in place, components that hardcode bg-white, text-green-500, dark:bg-zinc-900, etc. will still break in dark mode and violate the DS laws.

## What to Change
Scan src/ for these patterns and fix them:

### Pattern → Replacement mappings:
- bg-white → bg-background or bg-card (use bg-card inside cards/panels, bg-background for page sections)
- bg-zinc-900, bg-gray-900, bg-slate-900 → bg-background (dark contexts)
- bg-gray-50, bg-gray-100, bg-slate-50, bg-slate-100 → bg-muted or bg-card
- text-black → text-foreground
- text-white → text-primary-foreground (only when on bg-primary), otherwise text-foreground or text-card-foreground
- text-gray-*, text-zinc-*, text-slate-* → text-muted-foreground (secondary) or text-foreground (primary)
- text-green-* → text-success
- text-red-* → text-destructive
- text-yellow-*, text-amber-* → text-warning
- text-blue-* → text-info
- bg-green-* (light) → bg-success-light
- bg-red-* (light) → bg-error-light or bg-destructive/10
- bg-yellow-*, bg-amber-* (light) → bg-warning-light
- bg-blue-* (light) → bg-info-light
- dark:bg-* → REMOVE and use semantic token that handles both modes
- dark:text-* → REMOVE and use semantic token
- bg-[#...] or text-[#...] → appropriate semantic token
- border-gray-*, border-zinc-*, border-slate-* → border-border

### DO NOT change:
- src/components/ui/* (shadcn primitives — they already use semantic tokens)
- Any CSS modules (.module.css files)
- colors in chart data arrays (MUI chart props like color: '#xxxx' — those are data, not UI tokens)
- inline styles that are dynamically computed

## Edge Cases
- Some components do creative things with specific shades — use best judgment for the nearest semantic token
- If a replacement would change the visual significantly (e.g., a very specific brand color used as an accent), leave a TODO comment and note it in your DONE comment
- Do NOT remove dark: classes that control visibility, layout, or non-color properties (dark:hidden, dark:flex, dark:block are fine)

## How to Verify
1. yarn build passes with no new errors
2. grep -r 'bg-white\|bg-gray-\|bg-zinc-\|text-black\|dark:bg-\|dark:text-\|bg-\[#\|text-\[#' src/ --include='*.tsx' --include='*.ts' returns zero hits (excluding ui/ primitives and CSS modules)
3. Visual check: toggle dark mode — no white boxes or unreadable text remain

comments.author: pm
comments.text:
GROOMED: Audit and replace all hardcoded color violations in src/ (excluding src/components/ui/ and CSS modules) with Strata DS semantic tokens.

User story: As a developer, I want all hardcoded Tailwind color classes and hex values in src/ replaced with Strata DS semantic tokens so that dark mode works correctly via tokens alone and the codebase is fully DS-compliant.

Known in-scope item from 8x4.1 QA: summaryInfo.tsx MUI chart inline styles reference removed --foreground and --border CSS vars. These must be updated to var(--color-foreground) and var(--color-border) respectively so chart axis labels and gridlines render correctly in both modes.

Replacement mappings (full list in bead description). Priority hits:
- bg-white → bg-background or bg-card
- bg-gray-*/bg-slate-*/bg-zinc-* → bg-muted or bg-card
- text-black → text-foreground
- text-gray-*/text-zinc-*/text-slate-* → text-muted-foreground or text-foreground
- text-green-* → text-success
- text-red-* → text-destructive
- text-yellow-*/text-amber-* → text-warning
- text-blue-* → text-info
- bg-[#...]/text-[#...] → nearest semantic token
- dark:bg-*/dark:text-* (color only) → remove and use semantic token
- border-gray-*/border-zinc-* → border-border

Do NOT touch:
- src/components/ui/ (shadcn primitives)
- .module.css files
- MUI chart data color props (inline style color values in chart datasets are data, not UI tokens) — EXCEPT the removed --foreground/--border vars in summaryInfo.tsx which are UI tokens

Edge cases:
1. If a component uses a very specific shade that has no clean semantic mapping, use the nearest token and leave a // DS: closest token available comment.
2. dark: classes controlling layout/visibility (dark:hidden, dark:flex, dark:block) must NOT be removed — only color dark: classes.
3. Do NOT change inline style properties that are dynamically computed from JS variables.

How to verify:
1. yarn build exits 0.
2. grep -r 'bg-white\|bg-gray-\|bg-zinc-\|bg-slate-\|text-black\|dark:bg-\|dark:text-\|bg-\[#\|text-\[#' src/ --include='*.tsx' --include='*.ts' --exclude-dir=ui returns zero hits.
3. summaryInfo.tsx MUI chart axes and gridlines render with visible color in both light and dark mode.
4. No visual regressions on dashboard, call page, or sign-in when toggling theme.

comments.author: eng2
comments.text: PLAN: Audit and replace hardcoded color violations across src/ (excluding ui/ and CSS modules) with Strata DS semantic tokens. Key fix: summaryInfo.tsx MUI chart hsl(var(--foreground))/hsl(var(--border)) → var(--color-foreground)/var(--color-border). 1. summaryInfo.tsx — fix MUI chart CSS vars, replace all text-indigo-600 dark:text-primary → text-primary, color dots → success/warning/destructive. 2. utils.ts — getCEFRColor() remove all dark: variants, map colors to semantic tokens. 3. result/[callId]/page.tsx — fix dark:text-indigo-400, bg-white/20 in hero, skill card colors. 4. interviews/[interviewId]/page.tsx — all dark:text-primary/dark:bg-primary → strip dark: prefix. 5. interviews/test/page.tsx — border-slate-200 → border-border. 6. sideMenu.tsx — dark:bg-indigo-900/40 → bg-accent. 7. callInfo.tsx — dark: proctoring badges, integrity/sentiment colors, language feedback borders. 8. call/index.tsx — amber/yellow consent banner, permission status colors, error text. 9. feedbackForm.tsx — bg-indigo-600 → bg-primary. 10. tabSwitchPrevention.tsx, createInterviewerCard.tsx, interviewCard.tsx, questionAnswerCard.tsx, fileUpload.tsx, details.tsx, dataTable.tsx, editInterview.tsx, layouts. Files: summaryInfo.tsx, utils.ts, result page, interviews page, test page, sideMenu.tsx, callInfo.tsx, call/index.tsx, feedbackForm.tsx, tabSwitchPrevention.tsx, various dashboard components, layout files. Test: yarn build + grep check.

Key fixes: 1) summaryInfo.tsx MUI chart hsl(var(--foreground)) → var(--color-foreground) and hsl(var(--border)) → var(--color-border) (QA callout). 2) utils.ts getCEFRColor() — removed all dark: variants, replaced raw Tailwind colors with error-light/warning-light/success-light/info-light/primary tokens. 3) All text-indigo-600 dark:text-primary → text-primary across 8+ files. 4) All dark:bg-* and dark:text-* color classes removed (used semantic tokens instead). 5) All bg-gray-*/bg-slate-*/bg-zinc-* → bg-muted-foreground/bg-border/bg-accent. 6) Status dots (red/yellow/green/gray) → bg-destructive/bg-warning/bg-success/bg-muted-foreground. 7) All bg-indigo-600 text-white buttons → bg-primary text-primary-foreground. 8) Proctoring badges → text-destructive/text-warning with semantic backgrounds. 9) Language feedback section borders → border-primary/border-success/border-info/border-warning. 10) layouts, sign-in, interviewCard, questionAnswerCard, feedbackForm, tabSwitchPrevention, createInterviewerCard. Notes: A2 and B1 CEFR levels both map to bg-warning (no orange semantic token); Grammar/Vocabulary/Coherence use closest available tokens with DS comments. Verification: yarn build exits 0. FAIL: AC2 not met — text-indigo-*, bg-indigo-*, border-indigo-* violations remain across 11 files. The AC grep (bg-white|bg-gray-|bg-zinc-|...) returned zero hits but does not cover indigo/border-red patterns; adversarial grep found extensive remaining violations.

title: Replace globals.css and tailwind.config.ts with Strata DS --color-* tokens
description:
## User Story
As a developer, I want globals.css and tailwind.config.ts to use the Strata DS token namespace so that all semantic Tailwind classes (bg-primary, bg-background, etc.) resolve to the correct brand colors.

## Why
The current setup uses --primary = purple HSL (243 75% 59%) and hsl(var(--)) syntax. Strata DS requires --color-primary = lime #E6F993 and plain hex values in CSS vars. Every component that uses bg-primary currently gets the wrong color.

## What to Change

### globals.css — replace ENTIRE :root and .dark blocks with Strata DS tokens:

**:root (light)**
--color-background: #EBECEE
--color-foreground: #02060C
--color-card: #fafafa
--color-card-foreground: #02060C
--color-popover: #fafafa
--color-popover-foreground: #02060C
--color-primary: #E6F993
--color-primary-foreground: #02060C
--color-secondary: #fafafa
--color-secondary-foreground: #02060C
--color-muted: #fafafa
--color-muted-foreground: #959DA7
--color-accent: #fafafa
--color-accent-foreground: #02060C
--color-destructive: #E52D49
--color-destructive-foreground: #ffffff
--color-border: #D0D4D8
--color-input: #D0D4D8
--color-input-background: #fafafa
--color-ring: #959DA7
--color-success: #098400
--color-success-light: #e5f9e4
--color-warning: #b27d00
--color-warning-light: #fff5cd
--color-info: #2164d1
--color-info-light: #ecf4ff

--color-error-light: #ffe4e7
--color-ai: #8b5cf6
--color-ai-light: #ede9fe
--color-sidebar: #fafafa
--color-sidebar-foreground: #02060C
--color-sidebar-primary: #E6F993
--color-sidebar-accent: #EBECEE
--color-sidebar-accent-foreground: #02060C
--color-sidebar-border: #D0D4D8
--color-brand-50: #fdfee7
--color-brand-100: #f9fdc3
--color-brand-200: #f4fb89
--color-brand-300: #E6F993
--color-brand-400: #C3E433
--color-brand-500: #a3c414
--color-brand-600: #A0C114
--color-brand-700: #7a9410
--color-brand-800: #5c6f0f
--color-brand-900: #4a5810
--color-brand-950: #233502
--color-chart-1: #6366f1
--color-chart-2: #22c55e
--color-chart-3: #E52D49
--color-chart-4: #f59e0b
--color-chart-5: #818cf8
--radius: 0.5rem

**.dark block**

--color-foreground: #EBECEE
--color-card: #02060C
--color-card-foreground: #EBECEE
--color-popover: #02060C
--color-popover-foreground: #EBECEE
--color-primary: #C3E433
--color-primary-foreground: #02060C
--color-secondary: #141E2C
--color-secondary-foreground: #EBECEE
--color-muted: #141E2C
--color-muted-foreground: #959DA7
--color-accent: #141E2C
--color-accent-foreground: #EBECEE
--color-destructive: #E52D49
--color-destructive-foreground: #ffffff
--color-border: #141E2C
--color-input: #141E2C
--color-ring: #959DA7
--color-sidebar: #02060C
--color-sidebar-foreground: #EBECEE
--color-sidebar-primary: #C3E433
--color-sidebar-accent: #141E2C
--color-sidebar-accent-foreground: #EBECEE
--color-sidebar-border: #141E2C

### tailwind.config.ts — update ALL color mappings to use var(--color-*) instead of hsl(var(--)):

Every color entry should change from hsl(var(--foo)) to var(--color-foo).
Add new tokens not currently in config: success-light, warning-light, info, info-light, error, error-light, ai, ai-light, input (bg-input), brand scale (brand-50 through brand-950), chart-1 through chart-5, sidebar tokens use --color-sidebar-* prefix.

## Edge Cases
- Keep --radius: 0.5rem and the existing borderRadius/boxShadow/keyframes/animation entries unchanged
- Remove the old HSL variables entirely — do NOT keep them alongside the new ones (causes namespace conflict per LEY 6)
- The @layer base body/border rules stay as-is (they use bg-background and border-border which are semantic)

## How to Verify
1. yarn build passes
2. In browser: bg-primary button is lime green (#E6F993), not purple
3. Dark mode: bg-primary becomes #C3E433, bg-background becomes #02060C

User story: As a developer, I want globals.css and tailwind.config.ts to use the Strata DS token namespace so that all semantic Tailwind classes (bg-primary, bg-background, etc.) resolve to the correct brand colors in light and dark mode.

Edge cases:
1. --radius: 0.5rem must be preserved; borderRadius, boxShadow, keyframes, and animation entries in tailwind.config.ts must not be touched.
2. All old HSL variables (--primary, --background, --foreground, etc.) must be fully removed — do NOT keep alongside new --color-* tokens; dual-namespace causes silent resolution failures.
3. --color-input (#D0D4D8) drives border-input; --color-input-background (#fafafa) drives the fill. Eng must verify both are mapped so form inputs show correct border AND background — the existing spec lists them separately.
4. Dark mode is class-based (.dark on html), not @media (prefers-color-scheme). The .dark selector must remain a class selector — do not convert to a media query.
5. Vendor/third-party components using inline styles are out of scope; do not adjust them in this bead.

How to verify:

title: Implement Strata Design System tokens
description:
## Why
The project currently uses a legacy shadcn/radix token namespace (--primary = purple #27272a, HSL values) that conflicts with the Strata DS (--color-primary = lime #E6F993, hex values). Every component using bg-primary gets the wrong brand color. The Strata DS laws require semantic tokens, correct primary color (lime), and dark mode via tokens — not dark: classes.

## What Needs to Done
1. Replace globals.css CSS variables with Strata DS --color-* hex tokens (light + dark modes)
2. Update tailwind.config.ts to map semantic class names to var(--color-*) variables
3. Audit src/ for hardcoded color violations and fix to semantic tokens

## How to Verify
- bg-primary renders lime (#E6F993 light, #C3E433 dark)
- Dark mode still works correctly
- No bg-white, bg-gray-*, text-green-*, bg-[#...] or dark: color classes remain in src/
- yarn build passes cleanly

title: Fix: unauthenticated redirect to protected route shows blank loading page instead of sign-in
description:
## Why this issue exists

In Next.js 14.2+, calling `useSearchParams()` in a client component without a `<Suspense>` boundary causes a CSR bailout — the page content is excluded from the server-rendered HTML. When the middleware redirects an unauthenticated user from a protected route (e.g. /dashboard) to /sign-in?redirect=/dashboard, the server sends only the layout shell (with the ml-[200px] pt-[64px] wrapper div) but omits the sign-in form entirely. The page appears blank/loading until JavaScript hydrates, and if hydration is delayed or fails the user is stuck indefinitely.

## What to change

File: src/app/(client)/sign-in/[[...sign-in]]/page.tsx

1. Rename the existing `SignInPage` function to `SignInContent` (it stays a client component with all the same logic)
2. Add a lightweight `SignInFallback` component that renders the same outer shell (absolute top-0 left-0 z-50, bg-background, centered card) with just the title and subtitle — no button (the button needs the searchParams to know where to redirect after OAuth)
3. Export a new default `SignInPage` function that wraps `<SignInContent />` in `<Suspense fallback={<SignInFallback />}>`
4. Add `Suspense` to the import from 'react'

No other files need to change.

## Edge cases

- The fallback must use the same absolute positioning (top-0 left-0 z-50) as the real page so the layout's ml-[200px] offset does not show through
- The fallback should NOT include the Google button (it has no access to searchParams yet, so the redirect param would be missing)
- The existing `loading` state on the button ('Signing in...') is independent of this Suspense boundary and stays as-is

## How to verify

1. Sign out of the app (clear cookies if needed)
2. Navigate directly to /dashboard or /interviews/any-id in the browser
3. Middleware redirects to /sign-in?redirect=...
4. Sign-in form should appear immediately — no blank page, no indefinite spinner
5. Clicking 'Continue with Google' should still pass the correct redirect param through to /auth/callback
close_reason: QA passed all 5 AC, yarn build clean, committed and pushed as bf8ab48 on origin/main

User story: As an unauthenticated user who navigates directly to a protected route (e.g. /dashboard, /interviews/:id), I want the sign-in form to appear immediately when I am redirected to /sign-in, so that I am not stuck on a blank or indefinitely-loading page.

Acceptance criteria:
1. In src/app/(client)/sign-in/[[...sign-in]]/page.tsx, rename the existing SignInPage function to SignInContent (keep it a client component with all existing logic intact).
2. Add a SignInFallback component in the same file that renders the same outer shell (position: absolute, top-0 left-0 z-50, bg-background, card centered) showing only the title and subtitle — no Google button (searchParams not available yet in fallback).
3. Export a new default SignInPage function that wraps <SignInContent /> in <Suspense fallback={<SignInFallback />}>.
4. Add Suspense to the React import.

- Fallback MUST NOT include the Google OAuth button; the button depends on searchParams to build the redirect URI, and searchParams are unavailable until Suspense resolves.
- The existing button loading state ('Signing in...') is unrelated to this Suspense boundary and must remain unchanged.
- Redirect param (?redirect=/dashboard) must still be correctly read by SignInContent after hydration and passed to /auth/callback — verify this is not broken.

How to verify:

2. Navigate directly to /dashboard (or any protected route) in the browser — do not go to /sign-in first.
3. Middleware should redirect to /sign-in?redirect=/dashboard.

title: invitation_id always null on response — invitation lookup failing client-side
description:
As an operator, I want every response linked to its invitation so I can trace candidate responses back to the invitation that was sent.

Why: The invitation lookup in responses.service.ts uses getSupabaseClient() (anon-key browser client). Candidates are unauthenticated — the anon client silently fails or lacks the right context to query invitations at call-start time. The error is swallowed ({ data: invitation } discards the error field), so invitation_id is always null.

What to change:

The invitation lookup must move server-side where the admin client (service role key) can be used safely.

Option: Add a server API route GET /api/invitations/lookup?application_id=xxx that uses getSupabaseAdminClient() to query invitations by application_id and return the id. Then in the call component (src/components/call/index.tsx), before calling createResponse, fetch this endpoint to get the invitationId and pass it explicitly in the createResponse payload.

Steps:
1. Create src/app/api/invitations/lookup/route.ts
   - GET handler, reads application_id from query params
   - Uses getSupabaseAdminClient() to query invitations.select('id').eq('application_id', application_id).order('created_at', {ascending: false}).limit(1).maybeSingle()
   - Returns { invitation_id: string | null }
   - Returns 400 if no application_id param

2. In src/components/call/index.tsx, before the createResponse call (around line 646):
   - If applicationId is present, fetch /api/invitations/lookup?application_id=applicationId
   - Pass the returned invitation_id into createResponse payload
   - If fetch fails or returns null, pass invitation_id: null (do not block call start)

3. In src/services/responses.service.ts:
   - Remove the invitation lookup block (lines 7-17) — the caller now passes invitation_id directly
   - Keep the insert as-is (invitation_id will be in payload from the caller)

Edge cases:
- If the lookup API returns null (no matching invitation), proceed with invitation_id: null — do not block the call
- If the lookup API fetch throws, catch it and proceed with invitation_id: null — do not block the call
- Test responses and direct calls (no applicationId) must still work with invitation_id: null

How to verify:
- Start a call via a valid invitation link — query the response row after creation and confirm invitation_id matches the invitation id
- Start a test response — confirm invitation_id is NULL and no error is thrown
- Check the server log for the lookup endpoint — confirm it returns the correct invitation_id

comments.author: pm
comments.text:
GROOMED: Move the invitation lookup server-side via a new GET /api/invitations/lookup endpoint using the admin client, then pass invitation_id explicitly into createResponse from the call component. User story: As an operator, I want every response linked to its invitation so I can trace candidate responses back to the invitation that was sent. Root cause confirmed: the anon browser client used in responses.service.ts silently fails for unauthenticated candidates; the error field is discarded so invitation_id is always null.

AC — new route (src/app/api/invitations/lookup/route.ts):
- GET handler; reads application_id from query params.
- Returns 400 if application_id param is missing or empty.
- Uses getSupabaseAdminClient() — never the anon client — to query invitations.select('id').eq('application_id', application_id).order('created_at', {ascending: false}).limit(1).maybeSingle().
- Returns { invitation_id: string } when a match is found, { invitation_id: null } when no match.
- If application_id is present but not a valid UUID, let Supabase return no rows — respond with { invitation_id: null }; do not attempt to validate UUID format in the route.
- The route returns only the invitation id, not any other invitation fields — this minimises exposure from an unauthenticated lookup.
- This route lives at /api/invitations/lookup and does not conflict with the Supabase edge function proxy at /api/fn/[...path].

AC — call component (src/components/call/index.tsx, before createResponse around line 646):
- If applicationId is present, fetch /api/invitations/lookup?application_id=<applicationId> before calling createResponse.
- Pass the returned invitation_id (string or null) into the createResponse payload.
- If the fetch throws or the response is not ok, catch the error, set invitation_id to null, and proceed — do not block call start; do not show an error toast or any UI message to the candidate.
- If applicationId is absent (test response, direct call), skip the fetch entirely and pass invitation_id: null.

AC — responses.service.ts cleanup:
- Remove the existing invitation lookup block (lines 7-17); the caller now passes invitation_id directly in the payload.
- The insert itself is unchanged — invitation_id arrives pre-populated from the caller.

Edge cases:
- Lookup returns null → proceed with invitation_id: null, call starts normally, no user-visible error.
- Fetch throws (network error, 500) → same as above; swallow the error, proceed with null.
- No applicationId on the component → skip fetch entirely, invitation_id: null.
- Multiple invitations for the same application_id → the ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1 picks the most recent, consistent with the backfill tiebreaker specified in agentfoloup-abs.
- The admin client is used only server-side in the API route — it must never be imported or used in client-side component code.

How to verify:
- Start a call via a valid invitation link; after the call begins, query the response row and confirm invitation_id matches the invitation's id.
- Start a test response (no applicationId); confirm invitation_id IS NULL and no error appears in the console or UI.
- Call GET /api/invitations/lookup with no application_id param; confirm 400 response.
- Call GET /api/invitations/lookup with an application_id that has no matching invitation; confirm { invitation_id: null } and 200 status.
- Check server logs during a valid invitation call; confirm the lookup endpoint returns the correct invitation_id.
- Run yarn tsc --noEmit after all three file changes; confirm zero type errors (especially after removing the old lookup block from responses.service.ts).

title: Link response to invitation via invitation_id FK
description:
As an operator, I want every response to be linked to its invitation via a foreign key, so that I can trace each completed interview back to the specific invitation that was sent.

Why: The response table has no FK to invitations. The only shared field is application_id, but there is no enforced relational link. Without this, it is impossible to query which invitation produced which response, or enforce referential integrity.

What to change:

1. NEW MIGRATION FILE — src/supabase/migrations/<timestamp>_link_response_to_invitation.sql
   - Add column: ALTER TABLE response ADD COLUMN invitation_id uuid NULL REFERENCES invitations(id);
   - Backfill existing rows: UPDATE response r SET invitation_id = i.id FROM invitations i WHERE r.application_id = i.application_id AND r.invitation_id IS NULL;
   - Add index: CREATE INDEX idx_response_invitation_id ON response(invitation_id);
   - DO NOT edit any existing migration file.
   - Run with: supabase db push

2. UPDATE response creation in src/services/responses.service.ts and/or src/components/call/index.tsx
   - When createResponse is called with an application_id, look up the invitation by application_id to get its id, then include invitation_id in the insert payload.
   - If no invitation exists for the application_id (e.g. test responses, direct calls), leave invitation_id as NULL — do not crash.

3. UPDATE src/types/database.types.ts
   - Add invitation_id: string | null to response Row, Insert, and Update types.
   - Add the FK relationship entry under response.Relationships.

Edge cases:
- invitation_id must be nullable — test responses, direct calls, and legacy rows have no invitation.
- Backfill only where application_id matches — do not assume all responses have an invitation.
- If application_id is null on a response row, skip it in the backfill.
- The response creation flow must not fail if the invitation lookup returns nothing.

How to verify:
- supabase db push succeeds with no errors.
- SELECT COUNT(*) FROM response WHERE application_id IS NOT NULL AND invitation_id IS NULL returns 0 (all backfilled).
- Create a new response via a valid invitation flow and confirm invitation_id is populated on the row.
- Create a test response (is_test_response=true) and confirm invitation_id is NULL — no crash.

comments.author: pm
comments.text:
GROOMED: Add invitation_id FK to the response table, backfill via application_id join, and populate at call-start time. Confirming three operator-approved decisions: (1) invitation_id is nullable — test responses, direct calls, and legacy rows legitimately have none; (2) backfill via application_id join is correct — the join key is the only shared field between the two tables; (3) response creation must do a lookup by application_id before inserting and fail silently (NULL) if no match — the response must never be blocked by a missing invitation.

User story: As an operator, I want every response to be linked to its invitation via a foreign key, so that I can trace each completed interview back to the specific invitation that was sent.

Why: Without this FK, it is impossible to query which invitation produced which response or enforce referential integrity. The only shared field today is application_id, and that link is implicit, not enforced.

AC — migration (src/supabase/migrations/<timestamp>_link_response_to_invitation.sql):
- ADD COLUMN invitation_id uuid NULL REFERENCES invitations(id) ON DELETE SET NULL — ON DELETE SET NULL is required so that deleting an invitation does not block the delete or orphan the response row; do not use ON DELETE RESTRICT or the default NO ACTION.
- Backfill: if application_id is 1:1 with invitations in production data, the UPDATE ... FROM join is safe; if multiple invitations can exist for one application_id, the backfill MUST use a subquery that picks the most recently created invitation (ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1) — verify this assumption against production data before running the migration; use the safer subquery form if any doubt exists.
- Add index: CREATE INDEX idx_response_invitation_id ON response(invitation_id).
- Apply with: supabase db push (not any other migration command).

AC — response creation (responses.service.ts or call/index.tsx, whichever initiates the insert):
- Before creating the response, look up the invitation by application_id; if multiple invitations match, use the most recently created one (ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1).
- Include invitation_id in the insert payload when found.
- If the lookup returns nothing (no invitation for this application_id), set invitation_id to NULL and proceed — do not throw or return an error.
- The lookup must happen before the insert, not deferred.

AC — types (src/types/database.types.ts):
- Add invitation_id: string | null to response Row, Insert, and Update types.
- Add the FK relationship entry under response.Relationships pointing to invitations.id.

Edge cases:
- invitation_id nullable covers: test responses, direct calls, legacy rows, and any future non-invited flow — do not default to an empty string or throw if lookup returns null.
- If application_id is null on a response row, skip it in the backfill — do not attempt a join on a null key.
- ON DELETE SET NULL means existing response rows are preserved if an invitation is later deleted; this is intentional.
- The backfill tiebreaker (most recent invitation) must be consistent with the forward lookup tiebreaker used in response creation — they must use the same rule.
- Confirm no existing RLS policies on the response table reference invitation_id before the column exists — if any policy is broken by the schema change, it must be updated in the same migration.

How to verify:
- supabase db push completes with no errors.
- SELECT COUNT(*) FROM response WHERE application_id IS NOT NULL AND invitation_id IS NULL returns 0 after migration (all backfillable rows populated).
- Create a new response via a valid invitation flow and query the response row — confirm invitation_id matches the invitation's id.
- Create a test response (no invitation) — confirm invitation_id IS NULL and no error was thrown.
- Delete an invitation that has linked responses — confirm the response rows remain and invitation_id is set to NULL (ON DELETE SET NULL working).
- Run yarn tsc --noEmit (or equivalent) to confirm database.types.ts changes have no type errors.

No RLS changes needed — response table has no policies in any migration. invitations.application_id is UNIQUE so 1:1 is guaranteed, but using subquery form per GROOMED tiebreaker consistency rule. Apply with supabase db push. title: deepgram.listen.manage undefined in installed SDK — register-call always returns 500
description:
As a candidate, I want the interview call to start successfully, so that I can complete my interview.

Why: QA2 discovered during fnl.3 regression testing that deepgram.listen.manage is undefined in the currently installed @deepgram/sdk version. register-call depends on this method to generate a token. Since it is undefined, every call to the Deepgram token path throws, hits the inner catch block, and returns HTTP 500. This was pre-existing before the fnl.3 fix and is unrelated to it.

What to change:
- Identify the installed @deepgram/sdk version (check package.json / node_modules)
- Determine the correct API for token/key generation in that SDK version (deepgram.listen.manage may have moved or been renamed)
- Fix the call in src/app/api/register-call/route.ts to use the correct SDK method
- If the installed version does not support this API, upgrade to a version that does

Edge cases:
- Do not change any other part of register-call — only the Deepgram SDK method call
- Verify the fix does not break the development-mode path (mock response when no voice provider configured)
- Check if deepgram.service.ts uses any related SDK methods that may also be affected

How to verify:
- A POST to /api/register-call with a valid interviewId returns 200 with a valid access_token
- The inner catch block is not hit on the happy path
- Development mode (no VOICE_PROVIDER env var) still returns the mock response correctly
close_reason: Operator confirmed Deepgram is working fine. QA2 finding was likely due to missing VOICE_PROVIDER or Deepgram credentials in test environment — not a real SDK compatibility issue.

title: Fix null dereference on screenShareResult.stream in call/index.tsx
description:
As a candidate in an interview, I want screen sharing to not crash the call if the stream is null, so I don't lose my session.

Why: call/index.tsx:556 accesses screenShareResult.stream directly after getDisplayMedia, but stream can be null. TypeScript flags TS18047. If stream is null at runtime, the next line (.getVideoTracks()[0]) will throw, crashing the call.

What to change:
- src/components/call/index.tsx around line 556
- After the getDisplayMedia call, guard before using the stream:
  if (!screenShareResult.stream) { handle gracefully — log a warning and return early or show an error toast }
  screenShareStreamRef.current = screenShareResult.stream;
  const screenVideoTrack = screenShareResult.stream.getVideoTracks()[0];

Edge cases:
- If stream is null, the screen share failed silently — should surface an error to the user or at minimum log it.
- Do not break the happy path where stream is not null.

How to verify:
- yarn tsc --noEmit shows zero TS18047 errors for call/index.tsx.
- Screen sharing still works normally in browser testing.

comments.author: pm
comments.text: GROOMED: Add a null guard on screenShareResult.stream in call/index.tsx around line 556; if stream is null, show a visible error toast to the user and return early — a silent log is not sufficient because the user must know screen share failed. User story: As a candidate in an interview, I want screen sharing to not crash the call if the stream is null, so that I do not lose my active session. AC: (1) yarn tsc --noEmit produces zero TS18047 errors for call/index.tsx after the change; (2) when stream is null the call session remains active — it does not throw or crash — and the user sees an error message (toast or equivalent notification); (3) the happy path where stream is valid continues to work exactly as before; (4) getVideoTracks()[0] is never called if stream is null. Edge cases: if getDisplayMedia resolves with a null stream (browser-specific behaviour), the guard must catch it — do not assume a non-null assertion is safe here; if the user cancels the screen share dialog (DOMException), that is a separate rejection path and must also not crash the call; the screenShareStreamRef must not be assigned a null value. How to verify: (1) run yarn tsc --noEmit, confirm zero TS18047 errors for call/index.tsx; (2) start a call, initiate screen share with a valid display, confirm video track is shared normally; (3) simulate failure by denying the screen share permission in the browser — confirm the call page stays active, no uncaught exception appears in the console, and an error message is visible to the user; (4) confirm screenShareStreamRef.current is not set to null after a failed attempt.

title: Audit and fix all text visibility in dark mode across the full app
description:
## User Story As a user, I want every piece of text in the app to be clearly readable in dark mode so I can use the interface without straining to read low-contrast or invisible text. ## Why The broader dark mode changes fix backgrounds and surfaces, but text legibility needs an explicit audit pass. Hardcoded text colors like text-gray-400, text-gray-500, text-slate-500 that look fine on white backgrounds become near-invisible on dark surfaces. Placeholder text, labels, table headers, helper/secondary text, badge labels, breadcrumbs, empty states, and tooltip content are common failure points. ## What to Change Search the entire src/ directory for text color classes that are NOT dark-mode-aware and fix them. Key patterns to find and fix: ### Hardcoded gray text (too dark on dark bg) - text-gray-400, text-gray-500, text-gray-600, text-gray-700, text-gray-800, text-gray-900 - text-slate-400, text-slate-500, text-slate-600, text-slate-700 - text-neutral-400 to text-neutral-700 - text-zinc-400 to text-zinc-700 Fix: Replace with text-muted-foreground (for secondary text) or text-foreground (for primary text), OR add a dark: override — e.g. text-gray-600 dark:text-gray-300 ### Near-white text (invisible on dark bg) - text-white used on non-colored surfaces (not on colored buttons — those are fine) - text-gray-50, text-gray-100, text-gray-200 — invisible on dark bg Fix: Replace with text-foreground or add dark: variant ### Placeholder text in inputs - Input placeholders inherit from CSS but check if any custom placeholder: classes exist - placeholder:text-gray-400 → add dark:placeholder:text-gray-500 or use placeholder:text-muted-foreground ### Secondary / helper text - Any

, ,
close_reason: All dark mode theme alignment work complete and pushed to origin/main. agentfoloup-1la superseded by fgt.5.

Scope — search for and fix these class patterns:
- text-gray-{400-900}, text-slate-{400-700}, text-neutral-{400-700}, text-zinc-{400-700} without a dark: override → replace with text-muted-foreground or text-foreground, OR add dark:text-gray-300 / dark:text-gray-400 as appropriate
- text-white, text-gray-50, text-gray-100, text-gray-200 on non-colored surfaces (not on indigo/colored buttons) → replace with text-foreground or add dark: variant
- placeholder:text-gray-* → replace with placeholder:text-muted-foreground or add dark:placeholder: variant
- thead / th elements with hardcoded text-gray-* → text-muted-foreground
- Empty state message spans/paragraphs ("No results", "No interviews found") — text-muted-foreground
- Files to audit in order: src/components/ui/badge.tsx, label.tsx, input.tsx, textarea.tsx, tooltip.tsx, table.tsx; then src/components/dashboard/**; then src/app/(client)/**; then src/components/call/**; then navbar.tsx, sideMenu.tsx

Edge cases:
- Text on colored backgrounds (white on indigo button, white on primary) is intentional — do NOT change those; check whether the parent element has a colored bg- class before changing text-white
- text-primary, text-foreground, text-muted-foreground already use CSS vars and are already dark-safe — skip these
- text-gray-300 and lighter grays are generally fine on dark backgrounds — only fix gray-400 and darker without dark: overrides
- MUI chart internals are covered by fgt.3 — skip sx/fill/stroke inside chart components here
- Do not change font-size, font-weight, tracking, spacing — color classes only
- If a class is conditionally applied via cn() or clsx(), add the dark: variant inside the same conditional string — do not flatten dynamic classes

How to verify:

2. Dark mode ON → interview list page: table column headers, row text, empty state message all legible
3. Dark mode ON → interview detail page: filter labels, candidate name/email text, badge chip labels all legible
4. Dark mode ON → create interview popup: all form labels, helper text, placeholder text visible in inputs
5. Dark mode ON → call page and result page: all text visible with no dark-on-dark instances
6. Dark mode ON → navbar and sidebar: nav item labels, section group text, any secondary labels legible

title: Fix dark mode for candidate pages, info pages, filters, dropdowns, and MUI charts
description:
## User Story
As a user, I want the candidate listing page, interview detail view, analytics info page, filters, status dropdowns, and charts to all render correctly in dark mode so I can work comfortably without visual glitches.

## Why
These pages have the most hardcoded indigo/light colors in the codebase and are the most user-visible parts of the app. summaryInfo.tsx has 3 hardcoded dark:bg-indigo-950/40 instances that use a non-standard approach. dataTable.tsx candidate rows, status badge chips, and filter controls all need dark-safe styling. MUI charts don't switch to dark mode automatically.

## Files to Change

### src/components/dashboard/interview/summaryInfo.tsx
This is the largest file (~900+ lines). Key issues:
1. Lines with bg-indigo-100 dark:bg-indigo-950/40 — these are stat card icon backgrounds. Replace bg-indigo-100 dark:bg-indigo-950/40 with bg-primary/10 dark:bg-primary/20 (uses CSS var, dark-mode-aware)
2. Line with bg-indigo-50 dark:bg-indigo-950/30 — same pattern, replace with bg-primary/5 dark:bg-primary/10
3. Any other hardcoded text-indigo-600, text-indigo-700 — replace with text-primary
4. MUI chart dark mode: The charts use fill/stroke with hardcoded colors or var(--foreground). Check the sx prop on PieChart, BarChart, LineChart. Add dark mode sx overrides:
   ```tsx
   sx={{
     '& .MuiChartsAxis-tickLabel': { fill: 'hsl(var(--foreground))' },
     '& .MuiChartsAxis-line': { stroke: 'hsl(var(--border))' },
     '& .MuiChartsAxis-tick': { stroke: 'hsl(var(--border))' },
     '& .MuiChartsLegend-label': { fill: 'hsl(var(--foreground))' },
   }}
   ```
5. Any bg-white inside this file → bg-card or bg-background
6. Any text-gray-* without dark: → text-muted-foreground

### src/components/dashboard/interview/dataTable.tsx
This renders the candidate response table. Check:
1. Table rows — tr and td might have hardcoded bg-white or hover:bg-gray-50. Replace with bg-background hover:bg-accent/50
2. Status badge chips — if using hardcoded color classes (bg-green-100 text-green-800 etc.), add dark: variants:
   - SELECTED: bg-green-100 text-green-800 → add dark:bg-green-900/30 dark:text-green-400
   - NOT_SELECTED: bg-red-100 text-red-800 → add dark:bg-red-900/30 dark:text-red-400
   - POTENTIAL: bg-yellow-100 text-yellow-800 → add dark:bg-yellow-900/30 dark:text-yellow-400
   - NO_STATUS/default: bg-gray-100 text-gray-600 → add dark:bg-gray-800 dark:text-gray-400
3. Any border-gray-* → border-border
4. thead background if hardcoded

### src/app/(client)/interviews/[interviewId]/page.tsx
The interview detail view with filters. Check:
1. The filter bar (input for name/email/jobId, select for response type, select for status) — ensure bg-background dark:bg-background or just use CSS var tokens
2. The Select component for candidate status (ALL/SELECTED/POTENTIAL/NOT_SELECTED/NO_STATUS) — shadcn Select auto-adapts via CSS vars, but any custom wrapping divs need dark: treatment
3. Share button and edit button — check for hardcoded colors
4. Page header/title area — any bg-white → bg-background
5. Any hardcoded text or border colors

### src/components/dashboard/interview/create-popup/details.tsx
Check form fields, labels, and any container divs:
1. bg-white → bg-background or bg-card
2. input/textarea styles — should use CSS var tokens
3. Any text-gray-* → text-muted-foreground

### src/components/call/callInfo.tsx
The call info display with progress indicators:
1. Progress bar container — if using bg-gray-200 → add dark:bg-gray-700
2. Any stat display cards with hardcoded light colors
3. Camera feed area background if hardcoded

## Edge Cases
- shadcn/ui Table, Select, DropdownMenu all auto-adapt via CSS vars — only fix CUSTOM wrappers and styles around them
- MUI charts may have inline styles or sx props — adjust only sx-based styling, not inline style attributes
- Status badge colors: keep semantic meaning (green=selected, red=rejected) but add dark: variants with lower opacity/lighter text
- Don't change any data-fetching, state, or business logic — only className and sx strings

## How to Verify
1. Navigate to an interview detail page in dark mode — table rows should be dark, filters dark
2. Status badges (SELECTED/NOT_SELECTED/POTENTIAL) should be readable in dark mode
3. The summary analytics page should show dark-safe chart backgrounds and text
4. MUI charts should have readable axis labels and ticks in dark mode
5. Call info page in dark mode — progress and stats readable
6. Create interview popup — all fields visible in dark mode

title: Fix dark mode for modals, navbar, and sidebar components
description:
## User Story
As a user, I want modals, the navbar, and the sidebar to render correctly in dark mode so that the interface looks polished when dark theme is active.

## Why
These core layout and overlay components use hardcoded light-mode colors or indigo shades that don't adapt to dark mode. The sideMenu active state uses bg-indigo-200 without a proper dark counterpart (only dark:bg-indigo-800/60 — should use the theme token). The navbar dropdown and modal overlays need dark-safe styling.

## Files to Change

### src/components/sideMenu.tsx
Current active state classes: `bg-indigo-200 dark:bg-indigo-800/60`
Replace with: `bg-accent dark:bg-accent` (uses the CSS var which is dark-mode-aware)
Or keep indigo tint but use the standard dark variant that matches delivery framework: `bg-indigo-100 dark:bg-indigo-900/40`

Check the full file for any other hardcoded colors (white, gray-*, slate-*) that lack dark: variants. Add dark: classes where missing.

### src/components/navbar.tsx
Read the file and check:
- Any hardcoded bg-white or bg-gray-* classes — replace with bg-background or add dark: variant
- The user avatar dropdown trigger and dropdown content — ensure popover/card colors are dark-mode safe
- The ThemeToggle placement — verify it's visible in dark mode
- Any border colors not using border-border

### src/components/dashboard/Modal.tsx
Current overlay: likely uses bg-black/30 or similar
Check:
- The modal backdrop (overlay) — bg-black/50 or bg-black/30 is fine
- The modal panel/container — should use bg-background or bg-card, NOT bg-white
- Close button, title, and content areas — ensure text-foreground, not hardcoded dark colors
- Replace any bg-white, text-gray-* without dark: variant

### src/components/dashboard/interview/sharePopup.tsx
Read and check:
- Modal/dialog container — bg-background not bg-white
- Input fields — bg-input or bg-background with dark: if hardcoded
- Buttons — should use variant styles (secondary/outline) not hardcoded colors
- Any text-gray-* → text-muted-foreground

### src/components/dashboard/interview/editInterview.tsx
Uses AlertDialog from shadcn — check the wrapper and any custom content inside:
- If the AlertDialog content has custom bg/text — make dark-safe
- Form inputs inside — bg-background, text-foreground
- Action buttons — use semantic variant classes

### src/components/dashboard/interviewer/interviewerDetailsModal.tsx
Read and check:
- Image display area
- Slider controls styling
- Any hardcoded bg-white/bg-gray-100 → add dark: variant or switch to CSS var tokens

## Edge Cases
- Modals have a backdrop overlay — bg-black/50 is fine in both modes, no change needed
- Radix UI dialog/alert components have built-in dark support via CSS vars — only fix custom content INSIDE them
- Don't change any logic, only className strings

## How to Verify
1. Open the share modal in dark mode — background should be dark, text readable
2. Edit interview dialog in dark mode — form fields should show dark inputs
3. Sidebar active state should show a dark indigo/accent tone, not bright indigo-200
4. Navbar in dark mode — background dark, all text/icons visible
5. Interviewer details modal — all content dark-mode safe

title: Mix mic and system audio into screen recording
description: The screen recording currently captures video only (getDisplayMedia was called with audio:false). We need to also capture system audio from the screen share and mix it with the mic audio so the screen recording contains both voices. Update requestScreenShare() to call getDisplayMedia with audio:true to request system audio. In startScreenRecording(), mix the screen stream's audio tracks with the mic audio from the camera stream (same AudioContext mixing pattern used in startRecording()). The final screen recording must contain both the candidate mic audio and system/interviewer audio.

comments.author: pm
comments.text:
GROOMED

User story: As an assessor reviewing a screen recording, I want to hear both the candidate's voice and the AI interviewer's audio in the screen recording, so that the screen capture is a complete record of the session rather than silent video.

Why: A video-only screen recording is useful for detecting tab switches and open resources, but without audio the assessor must cross-reference it against the camera recording to match moments in time. Mixed audio makes the screen recording a self-contained artefact. The mic audio is already available from the camera stream; system audio is captured via getDisplayMedia with audio: true. Both are unused in the current screen recording.

What to change — two functions in src/hooks/useCameraRecording.ts and one caller update in src/components/call/index.tsx:

1. Add a second mixing context ref at the top of the hook alongside the existing mixingContextRef:
   screenMixingContextRef: useRef<AudioContext | null>(null)
   This is required because camera and screen recordings run concurrently and each needs its own AudioContext.

2. requestScreenShare(): change audio: false to audio: true. No other change to this function. The audio constraint is advisory — browsers that do not support system audio capture (Firefox) will return a stream with no audio tracks rather than rejecting the call. The displaySurface check is unaffected because it only reads the video track.

3. startScreenRecording(screenStream, cameraStream): add cameraStream as a second parameter (MediaStream | null, optional). Apply the same AudioContext mixing pattern used in startRecording():

   a. Create an AudioContext and a destination node. Store the context in screenMixingContextRef.current.
   b. If cameraStream has audio tracks (mic), connect cameraStream to the destination.
   c. If screenStream has audio tracks (system audio), connect screenStream to the destination.
   d. If at least one source was connected, construct the recording stream as:
      new MediaStream([...screenStream.getVideoTracks(), ...destination.stream.getAudioTracks()])
   e. If neither stream has audio tracks, fall back to recording screenStream as-is (video only — same as current behaviour). In this case close and null the AudioContext immediately; there is no point keeping it open with no inputs.
   f. Wrap the entire AudioContext section in try/catch. On failure, fall back to recording screenStream as-is and null screenMixingContextRef.current.

4. stopAndUploadScreen(): in the onstop callback, before or after uploadChunks(), close and null screenMixingContextRef if it is set:
   if (screenMixingContextRef.current) {
     screenMixingContextRef.current.close().catch(() => {});
     screenMixingContextRef.current = null;
   }
   This must happen in both the active path (onstop callback) and the inactive path (direct uploadChunks call) to avoid leaking the AudioContext if the recorder was stopped mid-interview by track.onended.

5. Caller in src/components/call/index.tsx: update the startScreenRecording call to pass the camera stream:
   startScreenRecording(screenShareStreamRef.current, stream)
   where stream is the MediaStream returned by requestCameraAccess() earlier in startConversation(). The camera stream must be passed here, not read from state, because the state update from setCameraStream is async and the ref may not be current at this point.

Edge cases:

- Browser denies system audio but grants screen video (user unchecks 'Share system audio' in Chrome's picker, or OS-level audio capture is blocked): getDisplayMedia resolves successfully. screenStream.getAudioTracks().length === 0. Step 3c is skipped; only mic audio (cameraStream) is connected to the destination. Recording contains screen video + mic audio only. This is correct behaviour — degrade gracefully rather than blocking the interview.

- Firefox and other browsers that ignore audio: true in getDisplayMedia: same outcome as above — stream has no audio tracks. No special handling needed.

- audio: true causes getDisplayMedia to reject on a specific browser/OS combination (not expected on current major browsers, but possible on older Safari or restricted enterprise environments): the existing catch in requestScreenShare() returns { stream: null, reason: 'denied' }. If eng observes this in practice, add a fallback retry inside requestScreenShare() — on rejection, try again with audio: false before returning reason: 'denied'. Do not implement the retry in this bead unless it is observed to be necessary.

- cameraStream is null when startScreenRecording is called (camera access failed earlier — should not happen because startConversation() returns early if camera fails, but guard anyway): skip connecting cameraStream to the destination. Only connect screenStream audio tracks if present. If neither is available, fall back to video-only.

- AudioContext construction throws (browser security policy, resource exhaustion): catch block records screenStream video-only and nulls screenMixingContextRef. Interview continues; recording is degraded but not broken.

- AudioContext is in suspended state after construction (browser autoplay policy): call context.resume() immediately after construction before connecting nodes. If resume() rejects, continue anyway — the nodes may still process audio once the context resumes on user interaction.

- Screen recording stops mid-interview (track.onended path, recorder goes inactive): screenMixingContextRef may still be open when stopAndUploadScreen is called in the inactive path. The inactive path calls uploadChunks() directly without going through onstop. Ensure the AudioContext close runs in the inactive path too, not just inside onstop.

- Two concurrent AudioContexts (camera and screen): each is independent. Closing one must not affect the other. The existing mixingContextRef and the new screenMixingContextRef are separate refs and must never be conflated.

- cameraStream audio tracks are shared between camera recording (startRecording) and screen recording (startScreenRecording): both AudioContexts read from the same physical mic track. Web Audio nodes from different AudioContexts can reference the same MediaStreamTrack without conflict — this is a valid use of the Web Audio API. No track cloning is needed.

How to verify:

1. Start an interview in Chrome on a platform that supports system audio capture (Windows or macOS with Chrome 74+). During the getDisplayMedia picker, ensure the 'Share system audio' checkbox is checked. Complete the interview.
2. In Supabase Storage, download the _screen.webm file. Open it in a media player. Confirm it contains both the candidate's voice and the AI interviewer's voice (not silent video).
3. Simultaneously, download the camera recording .webm. Confirm camera recording is unaffected — it still contains the mixed mic + AI audio as before this bead.
4. Repeat step 1 in Firefox. Confirm the interview starts and completes without error. The screen recording will be video + mic only (no system audio, because Firefox does not support it). Confirm this is silent-or-mic only (acceptable) and not a crash.
5. Repeat step 1 but uncheck 'Share system audio' in the Chrome picker. Confirm the interview starts. The screen recording should contain mic audio only (system audio not captured because the user declined it).
6. Inspect the browser DevTools Console during step 1: confirm no uncaught errors from AudioContext operations.

Resume context after construction. 4. stopAndUploadScreen: close/null screenMixingContextRef in BOTH active path (onstop) and inactive path (before uploadChunks). 5. call/index.tsx: pass stream as second arg to startScreenRecording. (4) closeScreenMixingContext() called in both active path (onstop, before uploadChunks) and inactive path (else branch, before uploadChunks). (6) mixingContextRef and startRecording() untouched — camera recording unaffected. Pre-mortem risks: (a) AudioContext autoplay policy may leave context suspended on some browsers — mitigated by resume() call; medium. (b) No error boundary if getAudioTracks() throws after mixing — extremely unlikely given MediaStream API guarantees; low.

title: Display screen recording alongside camera feed on frontend
description: In src/components/call/callInfo.tsx, show the screen recording video player alongside the existing camera recording player. Both players should appear in the response detail view. Handle the case where screen_recording_url is null (older responses with no screen recording).

comments.author: pm
comments.text:
GROOMED User story: As an assessor reviewing a completed interview, I want to watch the screen recording and the camera recording in the same view, so that I can cross-reference what the candidate was doing on screen with their facial expressions and body language without switching pages. Why: The screen recording data is now persisted (agentfoloup-1sz.3) but completely invisible to assessors — there is no player. Without this bead the screen recording epic delivers zero value to the end user, even though the full recording pipeline is working. This is the last step between stored data and assessor utility. What to change — one file: src/components/call/callInfo.tsx 1. State: add one new state variable alongside the existing recordingUrl: const [screenRecordingUrl, setScreenRecordingUrl] = useState(null); 2. Data fetch: in the fetchEmail useEffect (~line 106), read screen_recording_url from the response row alongside the existing recording_url read: setScreenRecordingUrl(response.screen_recording_url ?? null); No changes to the API call or any other fetch. 3. Layout: the two video players (camera and screen) must appear side by side when both are present, and the single player must appear alone when only one is present. To achieve this, wrap just the two video blocks in a flex-row sub-container inside the existing flex-col section (lines 310-353). Do not move the audio recording block — it stays above as a separate full-width block. Structure after the change:
{/* Audio — unchanged, full-width */} {call?.recording_url && (...audio block...)} {/* Video recordings — side by side when both present */} {(recordingUrl || screenRecordingUrl) && (
{recordingUrl && (...camera video block...)} {screenRecordingUrl && (...screen video block...)}
)}
4. Camera video block (existing, moved inside the flex-row wrapper): keep all content identical. Label stays 'Video Recording'. No other changes. 5. Screen video block (new, inside the flex-row wrapper):

Screen Recording

This is structurally identical to the camera video block — same className on the video element, same download link pattern. The only differences are the label text and the aria-label. Null state: when screen_recording_url is null (all responses recorded before this epic shipped), the screen video block simply does not render. No placeholder, skeleton, or 'recording unavailable' message is needed — the assessor sees only the camera recording, identical to the current experience. Do not add a fallback UI for the null case. Edge cases: - screen_recording_url is null (pre-epic response): screenRecordingUrl state is null, the screen block does not render, the flex-row wrapper collapses to show only the camera block at full available width. No visual change for existing responses. - screen_recording_url is null AND recording_url is null: the entire flex-row wrapper does not render (the outer OR condition is false). This is the same as today — no video section at all. - screen_recording_url is present but recording_url is null (camera upload failed): only the screen recording block renders inside the flex-row wrapper. No empty space is left where the camera recording would have been. - Both URLs present: two video players side by side with gap-4 between them. On narrow viewports, flex-wrap causes the screen recording to drop below the camera recording rather than overflowing. max-w-sm on each player ensures they do not expand beyond a readable width on wide screens. - Screen recording is a high-resolution desktop capture: max-w-sm (384px) is small for a full-desktop recording. This is intentional for the first iteration — the player is scrollable and the download link allows the assessor to view at full resolution. Do not add a resize/expand control in this bead. - Video format: screen recordings are .webm (Chrome) or .mp4 (Safari fallback), same as camera recordings. The mimeType inference (endsWith('.mp4') ? 'video/mp4' : 'video/webm') handles both cases identically to the camera block. - Stale data on tab switch: the fetchEmail useEffect depends on call_id. When the assessor navigates to a different response, setScreenRecordingUrl(null) is not called before the new fetch resolves. Add setScreenRecordingUrl(null) at the top of fetchEmail alongside the existing setEmail('') and setName('') resets to prevent the previous response's screen recording from briefly appearing while the new response loads. How to verify: 1. Open a response that has both a camera recording and a screen recording (recorded after agentfoloup-1sz.3 is deployed). Confirm both video players appear side by side under the audio recording. Confirm each has a labelled heading ('Video Recording' and 'Screen Recording'). Confirm each has a working download link. 2. Click play on the screen recording player. Confirm it plays video (not silent/blank) and contains the candidate's desktop. 3. Open a response that has only a camera recording (recorded before screen share epic, screen_recording_url is null). Confirm the camera player appears as before, no second player appears, no blank space or error is visible. 4. Open a response that has neither recording. Confirm neither player appears and no error is shown. 5. Navigate between two responses in rapid succession — one with a screen recording, one without. Confirm the screen recording player does not flash briefly when loading the response that has no screen recording. (Verifies the null reset on fetch start.)
