title: Upload screen recording via API and persist URL on response
description: Extend /api/upload-recording (or add a new route) to accept screen recordings. After upload, save the returned URL to the screen_recording_url column on the response row. Wire the stop-and-upload call into all interview exit paths alongside the existing camera stopAndUpload.

comments.author: pm
comments.text:
GROOMED

User story: As an assessor reviewing a completed interview, I want to watch the candidate's screen recording alongside the camera feed, so that I have full context for their behaviour during the session.

Why: Without the server-side changes in this bead, the screen recording blob is uploaded but stored under the wrong filename (overwriting the camera recording), and the URL is silently dropped by the PATCH whitelist before it reaches the database. The assessor sees nothing. This bead is the last gap between the raw recording and the data being retrievable.

What to change — two files, no new routes:

FILE 1: src/app/api/upload-recording/route.ts

Read the new optional FormData field type (string | null). Only the value 'screen' is meaningful; any other value (absent, empty, unknown) behaves as camera. Use type to compute the storage object name:
  - type === 'screen': fileName = 
  - otherwise: fileName =  (no change to existing behaviour)

The type value must not be interpolated directly into the filename without validation — only the two branches above are permitted. This prevents a caller from injecting path components (e.g. type = '../../etc').

No other changes to the upload route. mimeType validation, upsert behaviour, bucket name, and public URL construction are unchanged.

FILE 2: src/app/api/responses/[callId]/route.ts

Add 'screen_recording_url' to CANDIDATE_FIELDS (line 32). CANDIDATE_FIELDS controls which fields unauthenticated callers — i.e. candidates finishing their own interview — may write. The client (useCameraRecording hook, per agentfoloup-1sz.2) sends screen_recording_url in the same PATCH body as recording_url. Without adding it to this set, Object.fromEntries filtering silently drops the field and the column stays null.

CANDIDATE_FIELDS after the change:
  const CANDIDATE_FIELDS = new Set([
    'is_ended', 'tab_switch_count', 'fullscreen_exit_count',
    'proctoring_events', 'recording_url', 'screen_recording_url', 'details', 'duration',
  ]);

ADMIN_FIELDS inherits from CANDIDATE_FIELDS via spread and requires no separate change.

No other changes to the PATCH handler. The Supabase update call already uses the payload object built from the whitelist, so it will write screen_recording_url once the field is in the set and the column exists.

Edge cases:

- type param absent or empty (camera upload, existing call sites): fileName remains . — no behaviour change for camera recording. Zero regression risk.
- type param is exactly 'screen': fileName becomes _screen.. Camera and screen recordings for the same callId coexist in storage with different keys.
- type param is an unexpected string (not 'screen', not empty): treat as camera — fileName = .. Do not reject with 400; the caller is internal and this would be a bug in 1sz.2, not an attack. Log a warning for observability.
- callId contains path separators or special characters: callId comes from the client and is already used as the camera filename without sanitisation — this is a pre-existing concern outside the scope of this bead. Do not add new sanitisation only for screen recordings; apply it equally to both or raise a separate security bead.
- Upsert collision — screen recording re-uploaded for the same callId (crash recovery or retry): upsert: true overwrites the previous object. This is the same behaviour as camera recording and is acceptable.
- screen_recording_url value is null in the PATCH body (stopAndUploadScreen resolved null — zero chunks or upload failed): Supabase will write null to the column. This is correct — null means no recording available. The handler must not reject null values for this field.
- screen_recording_url sent by an unauthenticated caller with a malicious URL: the field is a TEXT column with no format constraint. This is a pre-existing pattern (recording_url has the same property). Out of scope for this bead.
- agentfoloup-1sz.1 not yet deployed (column does not exist): the PATCH will fail at the Supabase layer with a column-not-found error, which the handler returns as 500. This bead depends on 1sz.1 landing first; do not deploy 1sz.3 before 1sz.1.
- ADMIN_FIELDS whitelist: screen_recording_url is inherited automatically via the spread of CANDIDATE_FIELDS. Admins can therefore also write this field. This is correct — admins can do everything candidates can.

How to verify:

1. Camera upload regression: complete an interview without screen share. Confirm POST to /api/upload-recording (no type field) still returns a valid URL and the response row's recording_url is populated. Confirm the storage object is still named .webm (not suffixed).

2. Screen upload — storage key: complete an interview with screen share active. In DevTools Network, confirm POST to /api/upload-recording with FormData type: 'screen'. Confirm the response returns a URL containing _screen in the filename (e.g. call_abc_screen.webm). In Supabase Storage → interview-recordings bucket, confirm both .webm and _screen.webm exist as separate objects.

3. Screen upload — DB persistence: after interview end, query the response row: SELECT recording_url, screen_recording_url FROM response WHERE call_id = '<callId>'; Both columns must be non-null URLs. recording_url must point to the camera file; screen_recording_url must point to the _screen file.

4. Null screen_recording_url (no screen recording): end an interview where stopAndUploadScreen resolved null (e.g. zero chunks). The PATCH must succeed (200) and screen_recording_url must be null in the DB — not an error, not the string 'null'.

5. CANDIDATE_FIELDS gate: send a PATCH to /api/responses/ without authentication, with body { screen_recording_url: 'https://example.com/test.webm' }. Confirm the request returns 200 and the column is updated. Confirm that a field not in CANDIDATE_FIELDS (e.g. candidate_status) in the same body is still silently dropped.

title: Record screen share stream during interview
description: In src/hooks/useCameraRecording.ts (or a new hook), add a second MediaRecorder that records the screen share stream (screenShareStreamRef) independently from the camera feed. Start it when the interview starts, stop and upload it on interview end. Return the screen recording URL alongside the camera recording URL.

comments.author: pm
comments.text:
GROOMED

User story: As an assessor, I want to review a recording of the candidate's full screen alongside the camera feed, so that I can detect if they consulted unauthorized resources during the interview.

Why: The screen share enforcement (agentfoloup-bf5) guarantees the candidate is sharing their monitor, but without recording the stream the data is lost the moment the interview ends. Recording and uploading it closes the gap between real-time enforcement and post-hoc review. This is the core deliverable of the screen recording epic.

What to change:

1. In src/hooks/useCameraRecording.ts, add two new refs alongside the existing camera recorder refs:
   screenRecorderRef: useRef<MediaRecorder | null>(null)
   screenChunksRef: useRef<Blob[]>([])

2. Add startScreenRecording(screenStream: MediaStream): void to the hook. Mirrors startRecording() but simpler — no audio mixing (getDisplayMedia was called with audio: false, so the stream has no audio tracks):
   - Reset screenChunksRef.current = []
   - Select mimeType with the same MediaRecorder.isTypeSupported fallback chain as startRecording (webm;codecs=vp9 → webm → mp4)
   - Construct MediaRecorder from screenStream with that mimeType; fallback without mimeType if construction throws
   - Set recorder.ondataavailable to push non-empty chunks into screenChunksRef.current
   - Call recorder.start(5000) — same chunk interval as camera recording
   - Store in screenRecorderRef.current

3. Add stopAndUploadScreen(callId: string): Promise<string | null> to the hook. Mirrors stopAndUpload() with one important difference — it must handle the mid-interview-stopped case (track ended and recorder stopped automatically before this call):

   Active path (recorder.state !== 'inactive'):
   - Set recorder.onstop callback, call recorder.stop()
   - Inside onstop: stop screen share tracks (set t.onended = null before t.stop() per agentfoloup-ayu), upload chunks, resolve URL

   Inactive path (recorder.state === 'inactive', browser stopped the recorder when track ended):
   - Do not return null immediately — screenChunksRef may contain chunks from before the share stopped
   - If screenChunksRef.current.length === 0: resolve null
   - Otherwise: build blob from chunks and upload directly (same fetch to /api/upload-recording), resolve URL or null

   Upload call for both paths: POST to /api/upload-recording with FormData fields: file, callId, mimeType, type: 'screen'. The type: 'screen' field signals the server to name the stored object _screen.webm rather than .webm, avoiding a filename collision with the camera recording. Server-side acceptance of this param is covered by agentfoloup-1sz.3 — do not implement server changes in this bead.

4. In src/components/call/index.tsx, after the existing startRecording() call (~line 578), add:
   if (screenShareStreamRef.current) {
     startScreenRecording(screenShareStreamRef.current);
   }

5. In the updateInterview effect in src/components/call/index.tsx (the main interview-end path, ~line 651), add a parallel stopAndUploadScreen call alongside stopAndUpload:
   const [recordingUrl, screenRecordingUrl] = await Promise.all([
     stopAndUpload(callId),
     stopAndUploadScreen(callId),
   ]);
   Then include screen_recording_url: screenRecordingUrl in the PATCH body to /api/responses/. The DB column exists after agentfoloup-1sz.1; the server-side PATCH handler accepting this field is covered by agentfoloup-1sz.3.

6. In all other interview exit paths that call stopCamera() (the catch block in startConversation(), onEndCallClick if it does not go through updateInterview), also call stopAndUploadScreen(callId) or at minimum stop the screen share tracks cleanly. Do not leave a dangling screen share stream if the interview aborts.

Edge cases:

- screenShareStreamRef.current is null when startScreenRecording is called (screen share was denied or not yet implemented): guard with an if check; skip silently. Do not throw.
- Screen share stream ends mid-interview (candidate stops sharing, track.onended fires): the browser may stop the MediaRecorder automatically when its source track ends. stopAndUploadScreen must handle recorder.state === 'inactive' and still upload whatever chunks accumulated before the stream ended, as described in step 3.
- Zero chunks collected (interview was very short or recorder never received data): both paths resolve null rather than uploading an empty blob. Null is the correct value — do not upload a zero-byte file.
- MediaRecorder construction fails for screen stream (unsupported codec for the screen capture format): catch and fall back to no-mimeType construction same as startRecording. If both fail, log a warning and resolve null from stopAndUploadScreen without throwing.
- callId is empty string when stopAndUploadScreen is called: guard at the top of the function; if callId is falsy, resolve null and skip the upload fetch. An empty callId would produce an unnamed file on the server.
- Promise.all([stopAndUpload, stopAndUploadScreen]) — if one rejects, the other should not be abandoned: both functions must resolve (not reject) in all cases. Wrap any throw inside a try/catch that resolves null. The existing stopAndUpload already does this; stopAndUploadScreen must follow the same contract.
- Upload response is non-200 (server not yet updated for type: 'screen' param, i.e. 1sz.3 not yet deployed): resolve null rather than throwing. The PATCH will write screen_recording_url: null which is valid — no crash.
- Screen recording runs while camera recording fails: the two recorders are independent. A camera recording failure does not affect screen recording and vice versa.

How to verify:

1. Start an interview, grant camera/mic, select full screen share. Verify the interview starts normally.
2. Complete the interview by clicking End Interview. On redirect to the results page, open the Supabase response row for that call_id. Verify screen_recording_url is a non-null URL string (after agentfoloup-1sz.3 lands; until then it will be null even if upload succeeds, because the server does not yet persist it).
3. Before agentfoloup-1sz.3 lands, verify the upload at least fires: in browser DevTools Network tab, after ending the interview, confirm a POST to /api/upload-recording appears with FormData containing type: 'screen'. Confirm the request resolves 200 (or a predictable status if the server is not yet updated).
4. Verify camera recording is unaffected: the response row's recording_url must still be populated as before.
5. Mid-interview stop test: start an interview, click the browser Stop sharing button mid-interview, then end the interview normally. Verify stopAndUploadScreen still fires (check Network tab for the second upload-recording POST) and does not throw a console error.
6. Short interview test (end immediately after starting, before any 5-second chunk fires): verify zero-chunk path resolves null without error — no upload POST for screen recording, no crash.
7. Verify Promise.all parallelism: in DevTools Network, the two upload-recording POSTs (camera and screen) should appear at the same time, not sequentially.

title: DB migration: add screen_recording_url to responses table
description: Add a nullable screen_recording_url text column to the responses table to store the uploaded screen share recording URL alongside the existing camera recording URL.

comments.author: pm
comments.text:
GROOMED

User story: As an engineer implementing screen recording (agentfoloup-1sz.2 and agentfoloup-1sz.3), I need a column to persist the screen recording URL on the response row, so that assessors can retrieve and play back the screen recording alongside the existing camera recording.

Why: Without the column, agentfoloup-1sz.2 (recording) and agentfoloup-1sz.3 (upload and persist) are unimplementable. This is the foundation of the screen recording epic and blocks two P1 beads. The column must land before either of those beads is picked up.

What to change: Create a new migration file at supabase/migrations/20260514000001_add_screen_recording_url.sql. Never edit an existing migration file. The file content:

-- ============================================================
-- UP
-- ============================================================
ALTER TABLE response
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS screen_recording_url TEXT;

-- ============================================================
-- DOWN  (run manually to roll back — Supabase does not execute
--        this section automatically)
-- ============================================================
-- ALTER TABLE response DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS screen_recording_url;

The column is TEXT, nullable, no DEFAULT. NULL is the correct initial value for all existing rows — no recording exists for them. Do not add a NOT NULL constraint or a default string. The column name screen_recording_url mirrors the existing recording_url column (camera) and makes the distinction between the two URLs self-evident in queries and application code.

After applying the migration, regenerate TypeScript types so that the response type reflects the new column. Run: npx supabase gen types typescript --local > src/types/supabase.ts (or the project equivalent). This is required before agentfoloup-1sz.3 can type-safely write to the column.

Rollback plan: Supabase does not run the DOWN section automatically. To roll back manually: connect to the database and execute ALTER TABLE response DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS screen_recording_url; then delete the migration file and revert the generated types. Dropping a nullable TEXT column with no dependents is instantaneous and non-destructive.

Edge cases:
- Column already exists (migration applied twice or from a prior attempt): ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS is a no-op — no error, no data loss.
- Existing response rows: column is nullable with no DEFAULT, so all existing rows receive NULL. This is correct — there is no screen recording for any past interview.
- Name collision with recording_url: the new column is screen_recording_url, not recording_url. Both columns coexist on the same table. No rename or removal of recording_url is involved in this bead.
- Migration filename ordering: the latest migration is 20260506000001. The new file (20260514000001) is strictly later by date — Supabase applies migrations in lexicographic filename order. No ordering conflict.
- TypeScript types out of sync: if types are not regenerated after applying the migration, agentfoloup-1sz.3 will receive a type error when writing screen_recording_url. Type regeneration is part of this bead's definition of done.
- RLS: the response table's RLS posture is unchanged by this migration. No policy additions or modifications are needed for the new column — it inherits the same access rules as the rest of the table.

How to verify:
1. Apply the migration to the local Supabase instance: npx supabase db push (or npx supabase migration up).
2. Confirm the column exists: run SELECT column_name, data_type, is_nullable FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'response' AND column_name = 'screen_recording_url'; — expect one row: text, YES.
3. Confirm existing rows are unaffected: run SELECT COUNT(*) FROM response WHERE screen_recording_url IS NOT NULL; — expect 0.
4. Confirm the column accepts a URL value and null: run UPDATE response SET screen_recording_url = 'https://example.com/test.webm' WHERE id = (SELECT id FROM response LIMIT 1); then revert with UPDATE response SET screen_recording_url = NULL WHERE screen_recording_url = 'https://example.com/test.webm'; — both must succeed without error.
5. Confirm generated types include screen_recording_url: after running type generation, verify src/types/supabase.ts contains screen_recording_url: string | null in the response row type.
6. Apply the migration to staging and repeat steps 2-3.

title: Require full screen share after camera/mic access
description:
After camera and microphone access is granted in startConversation(), prompt the candidate for screen sharing. The share must be the entire screen (monitor), not a browser tab or window. If the candidate selects a tab or window instead of a full screen, reject it and re-prompt. If they deny screen share entirely, block the interview from starting.

## What to change
- src/components/call/index.tsx: after requestCameraAccess() succeeds, call getDisplayMedia to request screen share. Enforce displaySurface === 'monitor'. If candidate picks tab or window, show an error and re-prompt or block start.
- src/hooks/useCameraRecording.ts (or a new hook): add requestScreenShare() that calls navigator.mediaDevices.getDisplayMedia({ video: { displaySurface: 'monitor' }, audio: false }) and validates the track's displaySurface constraint.
- Stop screen share stream when the interview ends (alongside camera stream cleanup).

## Edge cases
- Candidate selects tab or window instead of monitor: reject and show clear error message
- Candidate cancels/denies screen share: block interview start with clear error message
- Browser does not support getDisplayMedia: handle gracefully
- Screen share stream ends mid-interview (candidate stops sharing): detect via track.onended and handle

## How to verify
- Start interview flow: after camera/mic granted, screen share prompt appears
- Selecting a tab or window is rejected with an error
- Selecting full screen proceeds to interview
- Denying screen share blocks interview start

comments.author: pm
comments.text:
GROOMED

User story: As an assessor, I want candidates to share their full monitor during the interview, so that I can verify they are not consulting unauthorized resources on other windows or applications — a tab-only share defeats the proctoring intent entirely.

Why: getDisplayMedia allows candidates to share a single browser tab, which means every other app on their desktop is invisible to the assessor. Full monitor share is the minimum surface that makes screen-share monitoring meaningful. Anything less is a false assurance that could let candidates cheat undetected while the assessor believes the session is monitored.

What to change:

1. Add requestScreenShare() to src/hooks/useCameraRecording.ts (or a new useScreenShare.ts hook following the same pattern). The function must:
   - Call navigator.mediaDevices.getDisplayMedia({ video: { displaySurface: 'monitor' }, audio: false }). The displaySurface hint steers the browser picker to pre-select monitor, but does not enforce it.
   - Read the returned video track's settings: track.getSettings().displaySurface.
   - If displaySurface is defined and is not 'monitor' (i.e. it is 'browser', 'application', or 'window'), stop all returned tracks immediately and return a typed failure: { stream: null, reason: 'wrong_surface' }.
   - If displaySurface is undefined (browser does not report it — older Firefox, some Chromium builds), allow the stream through. Do not block on unsupported browsers.
   - If getDisplayMedia rejects (user cancelled or denied): return { stream: null, reason: 'denied' }.
   - If the browser does not expose navigator.mediaDevices.getDisplayMedia: return { stream: null, reason: 'unsupported' }.
   - On success: attach track.onended handler before returning, or return the stream and let the caller attach the handler (caller pattern is preferred to keep the hook stateless).
   - Return { stream: MediaStream, reason: null } on success.

2. In startConversation() in src/components/call/index.tsx, after requestCameraAccess() succeeds (after line 504) and before fetching the interviewer/token:
   - Call requestScreenShare().
   - On reason 'denied': toast.error('Screen sharing is required to start the interview. Please allow it and try again.'); setLoading(false); return. Also stop camera stream (stopCamera()) to release the camera — do not leave a dangling stream.
   - On reason 'wrong_surface': toast.error('Please share your full screen (monitor), not a tab or window. Click Start Interview to try again.'); setLoading(false); stopCamera(); return.
   - On reason 'unsupported': toast.error('Your browser does not support screen sharing required for this interview. Please use Chrome or Firefox.'); setLoading(false); stopCamera(); return.
   - On success: store the screen share stream in a ref/state. Attach track.onended to the video track to detect mid-interview share-stop.
   - Add a fullscreen re-entry check immediately after requestScreenShare() resolves (the getDisplayMedia dialog also exits fullscreen, same as the camera dialog). This is in addition to the existing fallback at line 594.

3. track.onended handler (fires when candidate clicks the browser Stop sharing button mid-interview):
   - Log a proctoring event of type 'screen_share_ended' with timestamp to the proctoring events array (same pattern as tab switch events).
   - Show a warning dialog telling the candidate their screen share was stopped and to re-share or acknowledge (consistent with the TabSwitchWarning pattern — do NOT automatically end the interview, to avoid false positives from accidental dismissal).

4. On interview end (all exit paths — onEndCallClick, AgentEvents.Close, error catch block): stop screen share tracks alongside camera tracks.

Edge cases:

- Candidate selects a browser tab (displaySurface === 'browser' or 'application'): stop tracks, show wrong_surface error, return early, stop camera.
- Candidate selects an application window (displaySurface === 'window'): same treatment.
- Candidate clicks Cancel on screen share dialog (NotAllowedError): show denied error, return early, stop camera.
- Browser does not support getDisplayMedia (very old browser, some mobile): show unsupported error, return early, stop camera.
- Browser does not expose displaySurface in track.getSettings() (Firefox before v116, some Chromium builds): allow through without rejecting. We cannot enforce surface type on these browsers — blocking them would exclude candidates unfairly. Log nothing extra.
- Multiple monitors: candidate picks any single monitor — all monitors report displaySurface === 'monitor', so any monitor selection is valid. Do not restrict to a specific monitor.
- Screen share stream ends mid-interview (candidate clicks Stop sharing): track.onended fires, proctoring event logged, warning shown, interview continues. This mirrors how camera covered detection works — warn but do not force-end.
- isTestResponse mode: screen share is still required. The enforcement applies regardless of test mode, because QA needs to be able to test this flow.
- Camera access fails before screen share: requestCameraAccess() returns null, startConversation() already returns early — requestScreenShare() is never called. Correct — no change needed for this path.
- Camera stream is dangling if screen share fails: stopCamera() must be called on all three failure reasons (denied, wrong_surface, unsupported) before returning to prevent holding the camera open with no interview running.
- getDisplayMedia dialog also exits fullscreen: add a re-entry check immediately after screen share resolves, before proceeding to agent setup. The existing fallback at line 594 is a safety net but should not be the only recovery point.

How to verify:

1. Open the call page in Chrome. Enter email and name, click Start Interview.
2. Grant camera and microphone access.
3. The screen share picker appears. Select a browser tab → error toast appears ('Please share your full screen, not a tab or window'), loading spinner stops, interview does NOT start, camera is released (no camera indicator light on after dismissal).
4. Click Start Interview again. Select an application window → same rejection.
5. Click Start Interview again. Click Cancel on the screen share picker → error toast ('Screen sharing is required'), interview does NOT start.
6. Click Start Interview again. Select your full monitor → interview starts normally, page re-enters fullscreen.
7. During the interview, click the browser's Stop sharing button in the toolbar → a warning dialog appears (consistent with tab-switch warning style), interview does NOT end, the proctoring event 'screen_share_ended' is recorded.
8. End the interview normally → verify the screen share track is stopped (the browser's sharing indicator in the toolbar disappears).
9. Repeat steps 1-6 in Firefox. If Firefox does not report displaySurface: verify the interview starts regardless of what surface was picked (no false rejection).
10. Repeat step 5 (cancel screen share) and verify the camera light turns off — camera stream must be fully released on denial.

title: Re-enter fullscreen after camera/mic permission grant
description:
When a candidate clicks Start and the browser shows the camera/microphone permission dialog, the dialog causes the browser to exit fullscreen mode. After the user grants access, fullscreen is never re-entered.

## What to change
In src/components/call/index.tsx, inside startConversation():
1. After requestCameraAccess() succeeds and setPermissionStatus('granted') is called (~line 504), re-request fullscreen if document.fullscreenElement is null.
2. Add a second fullscreen check just before setIsStarted(true) (~line 588) as a fallback to ensure fullscreen is active when the test actually begins.

## How to verify
- Open call page, click start
- Grant camera/microphone access when browser prompts
- Verify page re-enters fullscreen mode immediately after permission is granted
- Verify that even if fullscreen somehow failed the first time, it is entered before the interview starts
close_reason: QA passed. Two fullscreen re-entry guards added in startConversation() — after permission grant and before interview start. Proctoring exit count verified clean at 0.

comments.author: eng1
comments.text: PLAN: Re-enter fullscreen after camera/mic permission grant. Two insertion points in startConversation() in src/components/call/index.tsx: 1. After setPermissionStatus('granted') (~line 504) — re-request fullscreen if document.fullscreenElement is null (permission dialog exits fullscreen). 2. Just before setIsStarted(true) (~line 588) — second fallback check to ensure fullscreen is active when interview begins. Pattern: same guard used at line 471. Files: src/components/call/index.tsx. Tests: manual — browser permission dialog flow.

comments.author: pm
comments.text:
GROOMED

User story: As a candidate, I want the interview page to stay in fullscreen after I grant camera and microphone access, so that I enter the proctored interview without the page dropping out of fullscreen and without having to manually re-enter it.

Why: When the browser shows the camera/mic permission dialog it forcibly exits fullscreen. Without re-entry the proctoring system logs a fullscreen exit at interview start — a false positive that unfairly penalises the candidate and corrupts the integrity data the hiring team relies on.

What to change: Two fullscreen re-entry checks must exist inside startConversation() in src/components/call/index.tsx:
1. Immediately after requestCameraAccess() resolves with a valid stream and setPermissionStatus('granted') is called (~line 504): re-request fullscreen if document.fullscreenElement is null. This is the primary re-entry point — it fires as soon as the dialog closes.
2. Just before setIsStarted(true) (~line 598): a second guard re-requests fullscreen if still not fullscreen. This is the fallback that catches any timing gap between check 1 and the interview actually starting.
Both checks must use the same guard pattern: document.fullscreenEnabled && !document.fullscreenElement, and must swallow the rejected promise with .catch(() => {}).

Edge cases:
- Permission denied: startConversation() returns early before either check is reached; no re-entry is attempted. The Start button is also disabled while status is denied, so this path is already blocked upstream.
- Browser does not support fullscreen (document.fullscreenEnabled === false): guard prevents the call; no error thrown.
- requestFullscreen() rejects (iframe restriction, user gesture timeout, browser policy): .catch(() => {}) swallows silently; interview still starts.
- Permission was already granted from a prior visit (no dialog shown): fullscreen is never exited; first requestFullscreen() at gesture time already succeeded; both re-entry checks hit !document.fullscreenElement === false and are no-ops.
- User takes a long time to grant permission (30+ seconds): re-entry fires correctly once the await resolves, regardless of how long the dialog was open.
- Already in fullscreen when re-entry check runs (browser re-entered automatically): !document.fullscreenElement guard prevents a duplicate call.
- Mobile browsers where fullscreen API is unavailable: fullscreenEnabled is false; both checks are skipped silently.

How to verify:
1. Open the call page in Chrome or Firefox on desktop. Enter a valid email and name.
2. Click Start Interview. When the camera/mic permission dialog appears, grant access.
3. Observe: immediately after the dialog closes the page is fullscreen. No manual re-entry required.
4. Check proctoring counters: at the moment setIsStarted(true) fires, fullscreen must be active. The fullscreen exit count in the proctoring data must be 0 at interview start (the dialog exit does not count as a proctored event because isStarted is false during startConversation()).
5. Repeat with camera/mic permission already granted (no dialog): fullscreen should persist uninterrupted from click through interview start.
6. Repeat in a sandboxed iframe where fullscreen is blocked: the page should not throw a JS error and the interview should still start.

(1) After setPermissionStatus('granted') — re-requests fullscreen if document.fullscreenElement is null, firing immediately when the permission dialog closes. (2) Just before setIsStarted(true) — fallback guard catches any timing gap. Both use the same pattern as line 471 with .catch(()=>{}) to swallow rejections. Build: yarn build exit 0. Commit: 08b539d

Checkpoint 1 confirmed at src/components/call/index.tsx:506-509 — fires immediately after setPermissionStatus('granted'), guard: document.fullscreenEnabled && !document.fullscreenElement, swallows rejection with .catch(()=>{}). Checkpoint 2 confirmed at lines 593-596 — fires immediately before setIsStarted(true), same guard pattern. Fullscreen exit count = 0 at interview start confirmed: proctoring hook only activates on isStarted && !isEnded (tabSwitchPrevention.tsx:103), both checkpoints fire before setIsStarted(true). Pre-granted path: both checkpoints are no-ops (fullscreenElement non-null). Permission denied path: early return before either checkpoint. Fullscreen blocked (sandboxed iframe): document.fullscreenEnabled=false skips both calls, no error thrown. No existing unit test suite to run. title: Fix fullscreen not re-entering after camera/mic permission grant
description:
## User Story
As a candidate, I want to stay in fullscreen mode throughout the interview, so that the proctoring system works correctly and my session is not interrupted.

## Why
When the browser prompts for camera/microphone access, it exits fullscreen mode. After the candidate grants permissions, fullscreen is not automatically re-entered. This breaks proctoring and causes a poor experience.

## What to change

### Fix 1 — Re-enter fullscreen after permissions are granted
In src/components/call/index.tsx, after requestCameraAccess() resolves successfully (permissions granted), call document.documentElement.requestFullscreen() to restore fullscreen.

### Fix 2 — Fullscreen guard before test starts
In src/components/call/tabSwitchPrevention.tsx (or wherever the interview start sequence runs), add a check: before the test begins (proctoring activates), verify document.fullscreenElement is set. If it is not, call requestFullscreen() as a fallback before proceeding.

## Edge Cases
- Permission prompt may have already exited fullscreen before the user clicks grant — re-entry must happen after the promise resolves, not before
- If requestFullscreen() throws (user agent restriction), catch and log — do not block the interview from starting
- On browsers that do not support fullscreen API, the guard check should be skipped gracefully
- If the user is already in fullscreen (permission was pre-granted), do not call requestFullscreen again

## How to Verify
1. Open a fresh browser session (no saved permissions) and navigate to a candidate interview link
2. Click Start Interview — browser prompts for camera/mic access
3. Grant permissions
4. Verify the page is in fullscreen mode after granting
5. Refresh and repeat — verify fullscreen is entered even if the permission prompt briefly exited it
6. Open browser with camera/mic already granted — verify fullscreen is entered normally on start
close_reason: Superseded by agentfoloup-abq which shipped fullscreen re-entry after camera/mic permission grant.

title: Fix fullscreen exiting when camera/mic permissions are granted
description:
## User Story
As a candidate taking an interview, I want to stay in fullscreen mode throughout the entire test, so that proctoring works correctly.

## Why
Fullscreen is entered when the candidate clicks 'Start Interview', but the browser's native camera/mic permission dialog immediately exits fullscreen. This means proctoring never actually runs in fullscreen, defeating its purpose.

## What to change
File: src/components/call/index.tsx

1. Pre-request camera/mic permissions on the pre-start screen — before the candidate clicks 'Start Interview'. When permissionStatus is 'unknown', automatically call requestCameraAccess() (or show a 'Grant Access' button that does so) so permissions are granted before the Start button is hit. No dialog will then appear after requestFullscreen() is called.

2. In startConversation(), keep the synchronous requestFullscreen() call (lines 471-473) at the top of the function (before any awaits) — this is correct. But since permissions will already be granted by step 1, no dialog will interrupt fullscreen.

File: src/components/call/tabSwitchPrevention.tsx

3. In the fullscreenchange handler (lines 102-116), in addition to showing the dialog, automatically call document.documentElement.requestFullscreen() to re-enter fullscreen immediately whenever it exits during an active test. This is the safety check.

## Edge cases
- If the candidate denies camera/mic on the pre-start screen, the Start Interview button remains disabled (already handled).
- If re-entering fullscreen fails silently (e.g. browser blocks it), the warning dialog is still shown — that is acceptable.
- The pre-grant step should not start the camera recording, only request permissions.

## How to verify
1. Open a candidate interview link
2. Before clicking Start Interview, the browser should prompt for camera/mic access (or it should already show as granted)
3. Click Start Interview — fullscreen is entered and stays entered (no exit flash)
4. During the interview, press Escape — fullscreen should automatically re-enter
5. proctoring_events in the DB should only log fullscreen_exit if the candidate deliberately and persistently exits
close_reason: Superseded by agentfoloup-abq which shipped fullscreen re-entry after camera/mic permission grant.

title: Revert domain whitelist from /api/feedback — candidates call this route
description:
## Why
zfz.1 added @agenticdream.com enforcement to /api/feedback as part of the internal-routes whitelist. However, candidates call /api/feedback at the end of their interview session to submit feedback. The auth guard breaks this for all candidates.

## What to change
File: src/app/api/feedback/route.ts

Remove the getAuthSession() + 401 guard that was added in commit 126a5dc. This route must remain fully public (no auth required), consistent with the other candidate-facing routes listed in the middleware publicRoutes.

Also add /api/feedback to the publicRoutes array in src/middleware.ts so it is consistently documented as public.

## Edge cases
- Internal users calling /api/feedback: they can still call it (no auth = open to all, same as before zfz.1)
- Candidates with no session cookie: must work — no auth required

## How to verify
1. Call POST /api/feedback without any auth cookie or header
2. EXPECT: normal response (not 401)
3. Verify /api/feedback is in the publicRoutes list in middleware.ts

User story: As a candidate completing an interview, I want to submit my feedback without being blocked by an auth check, so that my session feedback is recorded regardless of whether I have an @agenticdream.com account.

Acceptance criteria:

1. Remove auth guard from /api/feedback — src/app/api/feedback/route.ts:
   - Remove the getAuthSession() call and the 401 response block that was added in zfz.1 (commit 126a5dc).

   - No other logic in the route may be changed.

2. Add /api/feedback to publicRoutes — src/middleware.ts:
   - Add '/api/feedback' to the publicRoutes array (or equivalent public-route list) so it is consistently documented as a public, unauthenticated endpoint alongside other candidate-facing routes.
   - No other middleware logic may be changed.

Edge cases:
- Candidate with no session cookie or auth header: POST /api/feedback must return a normal success response (not 401).
- Internal @agenticdream.com user calling /api/feedback: still works — open to all callers, no regression.
- Any other /api/* route protected by zfz.1: must remain protected — this change is scoped exclusively to /api/feedback.

How to verify:

3. Open src/middleware.ts and confirm '/api/feedback' appears in the publicRoutes list.

title: Server-side: validate candidate email matches invitation before creating response
description:
## Why
The client-side email check (4iy.1) can be bypassed by calling the API directly. A server-side guard is needed so that even a direct API call to create a response fails if the submitted email doesn't match the invitation's candidate_email.

## What to change
File: Wherever responses are created — likely src/app/api/responses/route.ts or the edge function that handles response creation.

1. In the POST handler for response creation:
   - Extract application_id (or invitation_id) from the request body
   - Look up the invitation record to get candidate_email
   - Compare submitted email (lowercased) against invitation.candidate_email (lowercased)
   - Return HTTP 403 with { error: 'Email does not match invitation' } if mismatch
   - Only proceed to insert the response if emails match

2. If the response route does not currently have access to application_id/invitation_id, ensure it's included in the request body (the call component already sends applicationId).

## Edge cases
- application_id not found or invitation expired: return 404, not 403
- Email case mismatch: normalize both to lowercase before comparing
- Response for an interview with no invitation (test interview from dashboard): skip the validation if no application_id is provided

## How to verify
1. With a valid invitation, call the response creation API directly (curl/Postman) with the wrong email in the body
2. EXPECT: 403 response, no response record created in DB
3. Call with the correct email (exact match)
4. EXPECT: 200/201, response created normally
5. Call with correct email in different casing
6. EXPECT: 200/201 (case-insensitive pass)
close_reason: Operator confirmed existing response workflow must not be changed. Client-side email validation in 4iy.1 is sufficient.

comments.author: pm
comments.text:
GROOMED

User story: As an operator, I want the server to reject response creation when the submitted email does not match the invitation, so that the client-side check in 4iy.1 cannot be bypassed by a direct API call.

Acceptance criteria:

1. Email match guard — POST handler for response creation (src/app/api/responses/route.ts or equivalent):
   - Extract application_id (or invitation_id) from the request body.
   - Look up the invitation record to retrieve candidate_email.
   - Compare submitted email (lowercased, trimmed) against invitation.candidate_email (lowercased, trimmed).
   - If they do not match: return HTTP 403 with body { error: 'Email does not match invitation' }. Do not insert any record.
   - If they match: proceed with response creation as before.

2. Invitation not found or expired:
   - If the lookup for application_id returns no record or an expired record: return HTTP 404, not 403. Do not insert any record.

3. No application_id (test/dashboard interview):
   - If no application_id is present in the request body, skip the validation entirely and allow the response to be created as before.
   - This preserves existing behavior for non-invitation flows.

4. application_id already included in request body:
   - Confirm the call component sends applicationId in the response creation request. If it does not, this must be added as part of this bead so the server has the data it needs to look up the invitation.

Edge cases:
- Email case mismatch (JANE@EXAMPLE.COM vs jane@example.com): must pass — normalize both sides before comparing.
- Leading/trailing whitespace in submitted email: trim before comparing.
- application_id present but references a deleted invitation: return 404.
- Concurrent duplicate submissions (same applicationId, same email, race condition): guard must not create duplicate response records — rely on existing DB constraints or upsert logic.
- Malformed request body (missing email field entirely): return 400 before reaching the invitation lookup.

How to verify:
1. Obtain a valid invitation and its candidate_email.
2. Call the response creation endpoint directly (curl or Postman) with the correct applicationId but a wrong email in the body.
3. EXPECT: HTTP 403 response. Body contains { error: 'Email does not match invitation' }. No new record in the responses table.
4. Repeat with the correct email (exact match).
5. EXPECT: HTTP 200 or 201. Response record created in the DB.
6. Repeat with the correct email in a different case.
7. EXPECT: HTTP 200 or 201 (case-insensitive pass).
8. Call with no applicationId in the body.
9. EXPECT: response created normally (no regression for test interviews).

title: Call page: replace pre-filled email/name with required input form and validate against invitation
description:
## Why
The call page currently pre-fills candidate email and name from the invitation record (clients.context.tsx prefillEmail/prefillName), but both fields are editable via useState — anyone with the link can submit under a different identity. The fix removes pre-filling and requires the candidate to enter their own email, then validates it against invitation.candidate_email before allowing the interview to start.

## What to change
Files: src/app/(user)/call/[interviewId]/page.tsx, src/components/call/index.tsx

1. In src/app/(user)/call/[interviewId]/page.tsx (InterviewInterface component):
   - Stop passing prefillEmail and prefillName to <Call> — pass nothing (or empty string) for those props

2. In src/components/call/index.tsx (Call component):
   - Keep the email/name fields as user-entered state (no pre-fill)
   - Before the interview starts (on the pre-interview gate screen), show input fields for email and name — both required
   - On submit/start: validate entered email (lowercased) === invitation.candidate_email (lowercased)
   - If mismatch: show inline error — 'This interview was sent to a different email address. Please use the email you were invited with.'
   - Only proceed if email matches

3. The invitation object is already fetched (invitation.candidate_email is available in page.tsx) — pass it down to Call as a prop for validation purposes (read-only, not for pre-fill)

## Edge cases
- Candidate enters email with different casing (JOHN@EXAMPLE.COM vs john@example.com): normalize both to lowercase before comparing
- Empty email/name: standard required-field validation, do not call the match check
- Invitation has no candidate_name: name field is still shown but not validated against anything — any name is accepted
- Invitation not found or expired: existing 'not_found'/'expired' guard handles this before reaching the form

## How to verify
1. Open a valid invitation URL /call/{invitation-uuid}
2. EXPECT: email and name fields shown empty, no pre-fill
3. Enter a wrong email — EXPECT: inline error, cannot proceed
4. Enter the exact invited email — EXPECT: interview starts normally
5. Enter the invited email in different case — EXPECT: interview starts (case-insensitive match)

comments.author: pm
comments.text:
GROOMED

User story: As a candidate opening an interview link, I want to enter my own email and have it validated against the invitation, so that I cannot start an interview under someone else's identity and the hiring team gets accurate candidate records.

Acceptance criteria:

1. Remove pre-fill — src/app/(user)/call/[interviewId]/page.tsx:
   - Stop passing prefillEmail and prefillName to <Call>. Pass empty string (or omit the props entirely) for both fields.
   - The invitation object must still be fetched and its candidate_email passed to <Call> as a separate read-only prop (e.g. invitationEmail) for validation — not for display or pre-fill.

2. Required input form — src/components/call/index.tsx:
   - On the pre-interview gate screen, show an email field and a name field, both empty on load.
   - Both fields must be marked required. The start/submit button is disabled until both fields are non-empty.
   - No pre-fill from any source (invitation, localStorage, URL params).

3. Email validation on submit:
   - On submit/start, compare entered email (lowercased, trimmed) against invitationEmail (lowercased, trimmed).
   - If they do not match: show the inline error message exactly — 'This interview was sent to a different email address. Please use the email you were invited with.' The interview must not start.
   - If they match: proceed to start the interview as before.

4. Name field — not validated against invitation:
   - invitation.candidate_name (if present) must NOT be used to pre-fill or validate. Any non-empty name the candidate enters is accepted.

Edge cases:
- Case mismatch (e.g. JANE@EXAMPLE.COM vs jane@example.com): must pass — normalize both sides to lowercase before comparing.
- Leading/trailing whitespace in entered email: trim before comparing.
- Empty email or name submitted: standard required-field validation error; do not reach the email-match check.
- Invitation not found or expired: existing guard (not_found/expired screen) fires before the form is shown — form and validation logic never reached.
- Invitation has no candidate_name field: name field is still shown and required; any value is accepted.
- Candidate corrects the email after seeing the mismatch error: error clears on next submit attempt; no stale error state persists.

How to verify:
1. Open a valid invitation URL /call/{invitation-uuid}.
2. EXPECT: email and name fields are empty. No pre-filled values.
3. Leave email empty, click start. EXPECT: required-field error, no progress.
4. Enter a wrong email (not the invited address), click start. EXPECT: inline mismatch error shown, interview does not start.
5. Enter the exact invited email, enter any name, click start. EXPECT: interview starts normally.
6. Repeat step 5 with the invited email in a different case (e.g. all-caps). EXPECT: interview starts (case-insensitive match).
7. Confirm no regression: a test/dashboard interview with no invitation still works as before.

title: Candidate identity verification — require email entry and validate against invitation
description: Currently the call page pre-fills the candidate's email and name from the invitation record, but those fields are editable — anyone with the invitation URL can submit under any identity. The fix: remove pre-fill, prompt the candidate to enter their own email and name, and validate the entered email server-side against the invitation's candidate_email before allowing the interview to proceed or a response to be created. This ensures only the invited person can take the interview.

title: ClientProvider fetch /api/user does not check response status before parsing JSON
description:
## Why
clients.context.tsx line 37-38: fetch('/api/user') result is immediately .json()'d without checking res.ok. If /api/user returns 500 (DB error), the body is { error: '...' } — an object. The check 'if (!userData)' passes (truthy object) and setClient() is called with an error object as the user, silently corrupting client state.

## What to change
File: src/contexts/clients.context.tsx, after the fetch call:

1. Check res.ok (or res.status < 400) before calling .json()
2. If !res.ok: call signOut() and router.push('/sign-in?error=auth_failed') — same as user-not-found path
3. Wrap the entire fetch block in try/catch: on network error, call signOut() and redirect

Also wrap the entire handleAuthUser function body in try/catch so any unexpected error triggers signOut + redirect rather than silently crashing the component.

## Edge cases
- 200 with valid user: no change
- 200 with null (user not found): existing behavior preserved
- 500 DB error: now correctly signs out and redirects instead of setting corrupt client state
- Network failure (fetch throws): caught, user is signed out cleanly

## How to verify
- Mock /api/user to return 500
- Sign in with valid @agenticdream.com account
- EXPECT: clean redirect to /sign-in?error=auth_failed, no corrupt client state

comments.author: pm
comments.text:
GROOMED

User story: As a signed-in user, I want the app to handle API errors gracefully when loading my profile, so that a server-side failure never silently corrupts my client session with an error object.

Acceptance criteria:

1. Response status check — src/contexts/clients.context.tsx after the fetch('/api/user') call:
   - Before calling .json(), check res.ok (or res.status < 400).
   - If !res.ok: call supabase.auth.signOut() and router.push('/sign-in?error=auth_failed'). Do not call .json() or setClient() on this path.

2. Network error handling:
   - Wrap the fetch call (and .json() parse) in a try/catch.
   - On any thrown error (network failure, JSON parse failure): call supabase.auth.signOut() and router.push('/sign-in?error=auth_failed').

3. Full function guard:
   - Wrap the entire handleAuthUser function body in a top-level try/catch.
   - Any unexpected error that escapes inner handlers must also result in signOut + redirect, not a silent crash.

4. Existing paths preserved:
   - 200 with valid user object: no change — setClient() called as before.
   - 200 with null/undefined (user not found): existing signOut + redirect behavior preserved.

Edge cases:
- 500 DB error returning { error: '...' } body: now correctly redirects; must NOT call setClient() with the error object.
- 401 from domain-whitelist enforcement (zfz.1): treated as !res.ok — signOut + redirect.
- Network timeout or DNS failure (fetch throws): caught by try/catch — signOut + redirect.
- JSON parse failure on a malformed 200 response: caught by try/catch — signOut + redirect.
- User navigates away before fetch resolves: must not call setState or router.push after unmount.

How to verify:
1. Temporarily make /api/user return HTTP 500 with body { error: 'db_error' }.
2. Sign in with a valid @agenticdream.com account and complete the OAuth flow.
3. EXPECT: browser redirects to /sign-in?error=auth_failed. No corrupt client state. DevTools Network shows the 500, not a crash.
4. Restore /api/user to normal. Sign in again.
5. EXPECT: dashboard loads normally — no regression.

Wrap entire handleAuthUser body in outer try/catch (signOut + redirect on escape). 3. Wrap fetch + .json() in inner try/catch: on network/parse error, signOut + router.push('/sign-in?error=auth_failed'). 4. Add res.ok check before .json(): if !res.ok, signOut + router.push('/sign-in?error=auth_failed'), return. 5. Preserve existing 200-null path (signOut + unauthorized redirect) and 200-valid-user path (setClient). 6. Guard setClient and router.push calls with isMounted. Files: src/contexts/clients.context.tsx. Test: yarn build (no test files).

Network/parse error path: same redirect. Outer catch: same redirect. Existing 200-null and 200-valid-user paths unchanged. Build: ✓ Compiled successfully, all 26 static pages generated, exit 0. Commit: 7096076

AC2 (non-ok path): lines 44-46 call signOut() + router.push('/sign-in?error=auth_failed') with isMounted guard, then return. AC3 (network/parse error): inner try/catch lines 49-53 calls signOut() + same redirect on any thrown error including network failure or JSON.parse failure. AC4 (outer try/catch): lines 62-65 wrap entire handleAuthUser body — any unexpected escape triggers signOut + auth_failed redirect. AC5 (existing paths preserved): !userData path lines 55-59 → signOut + /sign-in?error=unauthorized unchanged; 200+valid user line 61 → if (isMounted) setClient(userData) unchanged. title: Navbar getSession missing error handling and refresh_token_not_found guard
description:
## Why
src/components/navbar.tsx calls supabase.auth.getSession() but never destructures the error field. If the refresh token is stale (refresh_token_not_found), the navbar silently proceeds with a null session but shows no error and doesn't clear the cookie — user sees a broken logged-in state until they take an action.

## What to change
File: src/components/navbar.tsx, around lines 38-42

1. Destructure error from getSession():
   const { data: { session }, error } = await supabase.auth.getSession()
2. On error.code === 'refresh_token_not_found': call signOut(), setUser(null), setLoading(false) and return
3. On any other error: setUser(null), setLoading(false) and return — log the error

## Edge cases
- Normal session: no change
- Expired token (refresh_token_not_found): clean sign-out, navbar shows logged-out state
- Other session errors: navbar shows logged-out state, no crash

## How to verify
- Set a garbage sb-refresh-token cookie, load any page with navbar
- EXPECT: navbar shows sign-in link, no console errors about undefined session

comments.author: pm
comments.text:
GROOMED

User story: As a user with a stale refresh token, I want the navbar to detect the invalid session and show me the logged-out state immediately, so that I am not stuck seeing a broken half-authenticated UI.

Acceptance criteria:

1. Error destructuring — src/components/navbar.tsx ~line 38:
   - Change the getSession call to: const { data: { session }, error } = await supabase.auth.getSession()
   - The error variable must be checked before using session.

2. refresh_token_not_found handling:
   - If error exists AND error.code === 'refresh_token_not_found': call supabase.auth.signOut(), set user to null, set loading to false, and return early.
   - No further processing must occur on this path.

3. Any other error handling:
   - If error exists for any other reason: set user to null, set loading to false, log the error (e.g. console.error), and return early.
   - Must not throw or crash the component.

4. Happy path unchanged:
   - If no error and session is non-null: existing behavior preserved exactly.
   - If no error and session is null (logged-out user): existing behavior preserved exactly.

Edge cases:
- Normal authenticated session: no change in behavior.
- Expired/revoked token (refresh_token_not_found): navbar shows sign-in link/state, no console error flood, stale cookie is cleared via signOut().
- Network error or other AuthApiError: navbar shows signed-out state, error logged once, no crash.
- Component unmounts before getSession resolves: must not call setState after unmount (guard with isMounted flag or cleanup).

How to verify:
1. Open DevTools → Application → Cookies. Set the Supabase refresh token cookie to a garbage value.
2. Load any page that includes the navbar.
3. EXPECT: navbar renders in logged-out state (shows sign-in link). No repeated AuthApiError in console. No broken UI showing a partial logged-in state.
4. Clear the garbage cookie and reload.
5. EXPECT: navbar renders normally (no regression for valid or missing session).

Destructure error from getSession: const { data: { session }, error } = await supabase.auth.getSession(). 3. If error.code === 'refresh_token_not_found': call supabase.auth.signOut(), setUser(null), setLoading(false), return. 4. title: Auth flow hardening — eliminate all remaining login edge cases
description: Comprehensive fix for all remaining gaps in the Google OAuth / Supabase auth flow identified during a full audit after the stale refresh token bug (agentfoloup-s5r). Goals: no silent auth failures, proper server-side security enforcement, and clear user-facing error messages for every failure path.

title: Google OAuth redirect loop caused by stale refresh token
description:
## Why this issue exists
When a user has an invalid or expired Supabase refresh token stored in their browser cookies, supabase.auth.getSession() tries to silently refresh it, gets back AuthApiError: Invalid Refresh Token: Refresh Token Not Found (400), and returns a null session. The middleware then treats the user as unauthenticated and redirects them to /sign-in. When the user clicks 'Continue with Google', the OAuth flow starts but the stale token cookies are never cleared, so after exchangeCodeForSession sets new tokens, the old invalid cookies interfere — or the session cookie is not readable by middleware on the redirect — and the user lands back on /sign-in again.

## Error observed
[AuthApiError: Invalid Refresh Token: Refresh Token Not Found] { status: 400, code: 'refresh_token_not_found' }

## Affected files (from investigation)
- src/app/auth/callback/route.ts — exchanges OAuth code for session
- src/middleware.ts — session guard that redirects to /sign-in
- src/contexts/clients.context.tsx — calls getSession() / onAuthStateChange, may emit the error

## What needs to change
1. In clients.context.tsx (and anywhere getSession is awaited): catch efresh_token_not_found errors and call supabase.auth.signOut() to fully clear stale cookies, then redirect to /sign-in for a fresh login.
2. In src/app/auth/callback/route.ts: after exchangeCodeForSession, verify the session was actually created; if it failed or is null, redirect to /sign-in?error=auth_failed instead of silently proceeding.
3. In src/middleware.ts: on any session-read error (not just missing session), clear cookies and redirect cleanly to /sign-in rather than looping.

## Edge cases
- User with no cookies at all (first visit) — should not be affected
- User whose token expired naturally vs. token that was revoked — both should get clean sign-out + redirect
- Non-@agenticdream.com Google accounts — still get blocked by domain whitelist (intentional)

## How to verify
1. Manually set an invalid sb-refresh-token cookie in DevTools
2. Navigate to /dashboard
3. Expect: clean redirect to /sign-in (no loop, no console error flood)
4. Click 'Continue with Google', complete auth
5. Expect: land on /dashboard with valid session and no errors in server logs

comments.author: pm
comments.text:
GROOMED

User story: As a returning user with a stale/revoked Google OAuth session, I want the app to cleanly clear my invalid token and let me log in again, so that I am never stuck in a redirect loop between /sign-in and /dashboard.

Acceptance criteria:

1. Stale token detection — clients.context.tsx (and any other call site that awaits getSession):
   - On AuthApiError with code 'refresh_token_not_found', call supabase.auth.signOut() to purge all session cookies, then redirect to /sign-in.
   - Must NOT loop: after signOut the redirect to /sign-in must be a terminal action, not retriggered by the same error.

2. OAuth callback hardening — src/app/auth/callback/route.ts:
   - After exchangeCodeForSession, check that session is non-null before proceeding.
   - If session is null or the exchange errored, redirect to /sign-in?error=auth_failed (not silently forward the user).

3. Middleware cleanup — src/middleware.ts:
   - On any session-read error (not only missing session), clear auth cookies and redirect cleanly to /sign-in.
   - Must not issue a redirect that causes the middleware to re-evaluate and loop.

Edge cases:
- First-visit user (no cookies) — no change in behavior; must not trigger signOut path.
- Naturally expired token vs. revoked token — both treated identically: signOut + clean redirect.
- Non-@agenticdream.com Google account — domain whitelist logic is unaffected; still blocked as before after a clean auth exchange.
- Race condition: user has two tabs open; signOut in one tab should not cause infinite-loop behavior in the other.
- exchangeCodeForSession network failure (transient) — should redirect to /sign-in?error=auth_failed, not crash or loop.

How to verify:
1. Open DevTools → Application → Cookies. Set sb-refresh-token (or equivalent Supabase cookie) to a garbage value.
2. Navigate to /dashboard.
3. EXPECT: single clean redirect to /sign-in. No redirect loop. No console flood of AuthApiError.
4. Click 'Continue with Google', complete the OAuth flow with a valid @agenticdream.com account.
5. EXPECT: land on /dashboard with an authenticated session. Server logs show no refresh_token_not_found errors.
6. Repeat step 1-3 with cookies fully deleted (first-visit simulation). EXPECT: same clean redirect, no errors.

Files: src/contexts/clients.context.tsx, src/app/auth/callback/route.ts, src/middleware.ts. Test: yarn build (no test runner configured; build acts as type-check gate).

No AC failures.

title: Fix 504 timeout on analytics generation in get-call route
description:
## User Story
As a call reviewer, I want analytics generation to complete without timing out, so that I can see interview results without a 504 error.

## Why
The LLM call in the analytics pipeline has no explicit timeout. The heavy model (openai.gpt-oss-safeguard-120b, 16k tokens) sometimes takes longer than the current implicit limit, producing a 504.

Failing call: call_1778195014547_42a9wgrdm

## What to Change
Set the fetch timeout on the LLM call to **60 seconds**.

- File: src/lib/llm-client.ts — add an AbortController with a 60s timeout to the fetch call in callLlmEdgeFunction()
- Also add export const maxDuration = 60 to src/app/api/get-call/route.ts so Vercel allows the full 60s

No timeout-guard wrapping, no graceful degradation fallback — just raise the timeout to 60s.

## Edge Cases
- AbortController must be cleaned up (clearTimeout) whether the fetch succeeds or fails
- Existing error handling in callLlmEdgeFunction must still propagate non-timeout errors normally

## How to Verify
1. POST /api/get-call with call_1778195014547_42a9wgrdm — should return 200 with analytics populated
2. No 504 in response
3. Confirm the fetch timeout is set to 60000ms in llm-client.ts
close_reason: 504 timeout resolved by increasing timeout limit.

Edge cases: already-has-analytics path unaffected; malformed JSON handling preserved; timeout must not persist partial analytics; retry-analysis cron must still heal calls with null analytics. How to verify: POST get-call with call_1778195014547_42a9wgrdm returns 200 (not 504); retry-analysis cron later populates analytics; unit test for timeout-guard path (mock 30s delay, assert 200 + null analytics within 26s).

Replace direct await of generateInterviewAnalytics with Promise.race against a 25s timeout sentinel. On timeout: log warn, set analyticsFailed=true, continue with analytics=null so retry-analysis cron heals it. No throw, no partial DB save. 3. Add jest + ts-jest devDeps and write unit test: mock generateInterviewAnalytics to never resolve, assert 200 + null analytics returned within 26s. title: summaryInfo dark mode: CEFR colors, unlabelled text, advanced analysis labels

A2: "bg-orange-100 dark:bg-orange-950/30 text-orange-800 dark:text-orange-400 border-orange-300 dark:border-orange-800",
B1: "bg-yellow-100 dark:bg-yellow-950/30 text-yellow-800 dark:text-yellow-400 border-yellow-300 dark:border-yellow-800",

title: Dark theme: MUI BarChart and LineChart axis labels black in dark mode
description:
## Why
summaryInfo.tsx has a BarChart (line 822, score distribution) and a LineChart (line 920, score trends) with no sx prop. MUI charts default to black axis tick labels and legend text — invisible on a dark background.

## What to Change
File: src/components/dashboard/interview/summaryInfo.tsx

### BarChart (line 822)
Add an sx prop:
```jsx
sx={{
  '& .MuiChartsAxis-tickLabel': { fill: 'var(--foreground) !important' },
  '& .MuiChartsAxis-line': { stroke: 'var(--border) !important' },
  '& .MuiChartsAxis-tick': { stroke: 'var(--border) !important' },
}}
```

### LineChart (line 920)
Add an sx prop with the same axis overrides PLUS legend text (LineChart renders a legend):
```jsx
sx={{
  '& .MuiChartsAxis-tickLabel': { fill: 'var(--foreground) !important' },
  '& .MuiChartsAxis-line': { stroke: 'var(--border) !important' },
  '& .MuiChartsAxis-tick': { stroke: 'var(--border) !important' },
  '& .MuiChartsLegend-series text': { fill: 'var(--foreground) !important' },
}}
```

## Edge Cases
- Series colors (#4F46E5 indigo, #9ca3af gray) are data colors — leave them
- The two PieCharts already have the legend text fix from a previous bead — do not touch them
- No logic changes — sx prop additions only

## How to Verify
1. Enable dark mode, open the overall analysis view for an interview with response data
2. Score Distribution bar chart: x-axis labels (0-10, 10-20 etc) and y-axis labels are visible (not black)
3. Score Trends line chart: x-axis date labels, y-axis score labels, and legend (Average Score / Overall Average) are all visible

title: summaryInfo dark mode: candidate metrics labels, sentiment chart, top performers bg
description:
## User Story
As an admin in dark mode, I want the candidate metrics, candidate sentiment, and top performers sections to be fully readable so I can analyse data without squinting.

## Why
Three concrete issues remain in summaryInfo.tsx after the previous fix:
1. bg-indigo-50/bg-indigo-100 are light-mode-only backgrounds — they appear as bright white/light boxes in dark mode
2. MUI PieChart legend text has no dark mode color override — it renders black and disappears on a dark background
3. text-muted-foreground on metric card labels is too dim in dark mode — operator wants those labels clearly visible

## What to Change
File: src/components/dashboard/interview/summaryInfo.tsx

### Fix 1 — Metric card backgrounds (Candidate Metrics section, ~lines 455, 465)
- bg-indigo-100 → bg-indigo-100 dark:bg-indigo-950/40
These appear on the completion rate and average duration stat cards.

### Fix 2 — Top performer row background (Top Performers section, line 866)
- bg-indigo-50 → bg-indigo-50 dark:bg-indigo-950/30
Each performer row uses this as its card background.

### Fix 3 — MUI PieChart legend text (Candidate Sentiment section, lines 480-483)
The sx prop currently only sets fontSize. Add a fill/color override so the legend text is visible in dark mode:
```js
sx={{
  "& .MuiChartsLegend-series text": {
    fontSize: "0.8rem !important",
    fill: "var(--foreground) !important",
  },
}}
```
Use the CSS variable --foreground so it adapts to both light and dark mode.

### Fix 4 — Metric label text visibility (Candidate Metrics section, lines 596, 601, 605, 610, 614, 617, 621, 626)
text-muted-foreground on metric labels ("Avg Answer Length", "Relevance Score", "Depth Score", "Consistency Score" and their unit spans like "words", "/10") is too dim in dark mode. Change label p tags to text-foreground and keep unit spans as text-muted-foreground so there is a visible hierarchy.

## Edge Cases
- Medal rank circles (bg-yellow-500, bg-gray-400, bg-orange-600 with text-white) — leave them, saturated colors work in both modes
- Indigo text (text-indigo-600) — leave it, reads well in dark
- PieChart data colors (#22c55e, #eab308, #eb4444) — leave them, these are chart slice colors not text
- No logic changes — styling only

## How to Verify
1. Enable dark mode and open the summary/analysis view for an interview
2. Candidate metrics: all label text ("Avg Answer Length" etc) clearly readable
3. Candidate sentiment: pie chart legend text visible (not black on dark)
4. Top performers: performer rows have dark-aware background, names and scores readable

title: Dark theme incomplete on /interviews/[id] — details, responses panel, and text readability
description:
## User Story
As an admin using dark mode, I want the interviews detail page to be fully dark-themed with readable text, so I can review responses comfortably in low-light conditions.

## Why
The /interviews/[interviewId] page was partially converted in bz9.3 but several sections were missed. The responses panel and interview details sidebar still have hardcoded light-mode colors, and text colors are not readable enough in dark mode.

## What to Change

### File: src/app/(client)/interviews/[interviewId]/page.tsx

**Filter panel (line 371):**
- border-slate-100 → border-border

**Filter dropdowns and inputs (lines 378, 392, 423, 442, 453):**
- bg-slate-100 → bg-secondary on SelectTrigger and input elements

**Inactive toggle (line 365):**
- bg-[#E6E7EB] → bg-secondary (hardcoded hex, breaks dark mode)

**Tooltip backgrounds (lines 310, 325, 340, 355):**
- bg-zinc-300 → bg-popover (with text-popover-foreground)

**Response list hover/selected states (lines 465-466):**
- hover:bg-indigo-100 → hover:bg-accent
- bg-indigo-200 → bg-accent
- border-indigo-100 → border-border (or keep indigo as brand but add dark: variant for readability)

**Pagination (lines 529, 537):**
- hover:bg-slate-100 → hover:bg-accent

**Muted text (lines 521, 535):**
- text-gray-500 → text-muted-foreground

**Score tooltip (lines 508-509):**
- bg-gray-500 → bg-popover
- text-white → text-popover-foreground

### File: src/components/call/callInfo.tsx
Scan the full file for any hardcoded bg-white, text-black, text-gray-*, bg-gray-*, bg-slate-*, border-gray-*, border-slate-* and replace with semantic Tailwind classes. This is the interview details panel shown when clicking a response.

## Edge Cases
- Status indicator dots (red/yellow/green/gray circles) are intentional brand colors — leave them
- indigo brand colors on active elements can stay if they read well in dark; add dark: variant only if unreadable
- Do not change any logic, filters, or data display — color classes only

## How to Verify
1. Enable dark mode, open /interviews/[interviewId]
2. Filter panel, dropdowns, inputs all dark-themed
3. Response list items readable — hover and selected states visible
4. Click a response — details panel (callInfo) fully dark-themed
5. All text readable without squinting — no light gray on dark gray

title: Fix post-test redirect: admin to all-responses, candidate to individual result
description:
## User Story
As an admin completing a test run, I want to be redirected to the interview's all-responses page, not my individual result, so I can immediately see how the test performed across all responses.

As a candidate completing a test, I want to be redirected to my individual result page after results are compiled, so I can review my performance.

## Why
Admins are currently redirected to `/result/[callId]` after completing a test — the same page candidates see. This is wrong because admins don't care about their own result; they care about the aggregate responses view for the interview. The current behavior wastes the admin's time and may cause confusion.

## What to Change
File: `src/components/call/index.tsx`, line 674

Current code:
```ts
window.location.href = `/result/${callId}`;
```

The `Call` component already receives `isTestResponse` (line 55) and `interview` props. When `isTestResponse === true` (admin test run), redirect to the interview's all-responses page (`/interviews/[interviewId]`) instead. When `isTestResponse === false` (candidate), keep redirecting to `/result/[callId]`.

Check that `interview.id` is the correct field for the interviewId — verify against the interview type.

## Edge Cases
- If `interview.id` is undefined, log a warning and fall back to `/result/${callId}` rather than redirecting to a broken URL
- The fix is scoped to the single redirect at line 674; do not touch the duplicate-check redirect at line 353 or the check-response redirect at line 477

## How to Verify
1. Log in as admin, open an interview, click 'Test' to run through the interview
2. After completion: should land on `/interviews/[interviewId]` (all-responses page), NOT `/result/[callId]`
3. Open the same interview link as a candidate (no auth / candidate session)
4. After completion: should land on `/result/[callId]`
5. Confirm no regressions on lines 353 and 477 redirects

How to verify: (1) Admin completes test → lands on /interviews/[interviewId]; (2) Candidate completes test → lands on /result/[callId]; (3) no regressions on other redirect paths.

title: Fix webpack ENOENT cache errors and remove mobile browser targets
description:
## User Story
As a developer, I want the Next.js dev server to serve static chunks correctly, so that the app loads in the browser without 404 errors.

## Why
The dev server compiles successfully but all static chunk requests (webpack.js, main-app.js, app-pages-internals.js, layout.css etc.) return 404. Root cause is a corrupted .next/cache/webpack directory — some cache files have a trailing underscore extension (.pack.gz_) indicating interrupted writes. Webpack cannot read these and throws ENOENT unhandledRejection errors, which causes the client bundle to fail to serve. Additionally, the browserslist data is 15 months out of date and the build currently targets mobile browsers unnecessarily, adding compilation overhead.

## What to change
1. Delete the entire .next directory at C:\agentic-dream\internal-tools\agentfoloup\.next to wipe the corrupted cache and force a clean recompile.
2. Add a browserslist config to package.json that targets only modern desktop/evergreen browsers and explicitly excludes mobile. Use this:
   {
     "production": ["chrome >= 90", "firefox >= 90", "edge >= 90", "safari >= 14"],
     "development": ["last 1 chrome version", "last 1 firefox version", "last 1 safari version"]
   }
3. Run npx update-browserslist-db@latest to refresh the caniuse-lite data.
4. Restart the dev server and confirm all chunks load (no 404s, no ENOENT errors).

## Edge cases
- Do not delete node_modules or .env files — only .next.
- The browserslist production targets must not include any android, ios_saf, op_mini, or IE entries.
- If the dev server still shows 404s after clearing .next, check whether next dev is being invoked from the wrong working directory.

## How to verify
- Run npm run dev (or yarn dev) from C:\agentic-dream\internal-tools\agentfoloup
- Navigate to http://localhost:3001 — the page must load without browser console errors
- Dev server logs must show no ENOENT unhandledRejection errors
- Dev server logs must show no 404s for /_next/static/chunks/ paths
- The browserslist warning about stale data must be gone
close_reason: Fix complete — .next cleared, browserslist config added, build clean.

Add browserslist config to package.json (chrome/firefox/edge >= 90, safari >= 14 for production; last 1 version for development — no mobile targets). 3. Run npx update-browserslist-db@latest to refresh caniuse-lite. 4. Restart dev server and verify no ENOENT errors, no 404s on /_next/static/chunks/. Files: package.json. Test: start dev server, navigate to http://localhost:3001, confirm clean load.
